@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.heritages.Item.t0OfbPwWPYSr7JL5]{寻思默思族}

As Akiton's core cooled and its atmosphere thinned, contemplatives' distant ancestors sought shelter in underground sanctuaries. Life underground was incredibly different than on the surface, and though today no one knows what the first contemplatives might have looked like or what their cultural traditions might have been, those who weathered their planet's changes realized they would need to change to adapt to the new paradigm. And thus contemplatives turned away from the laborious routines of farming, hunting, and building on the surface. Instead, they began to explore a new frontier: the esoteric realms of the mind.

Through magically guided evolution, each generation grew larger brains and frailer bodies, dedicating themselves to the pursuit of knowledge rather than the struggle for survival. Today, contemplatives are hyper-specialized thinkers, able to unravel secrets at a whim and reflexively generate psychic forces. Space travel has lured many of the reclusive philosophers from their isolated Akitonian communes, promising them countless discoveries and data beyond the stars.

If you want to play a mental powerhouse with innate psychic abilities, you should play a contemplative.

You Might...

  • Overcomplicate challenges, often just to test your abilities and hypotheses.
  • Dislike pointless noise and mayhem that distracts you from deeper thought.
  • Dedicate yourself to mastering some great mystery, magical tradition, or field of study.
  • Refer to yourself as we, not I.

Others Probably...

  • Assume you know almost everything about anything.
  • Are reticent to voice their thoughts around you, believing you'll relentlessly lecture them or question their epistemological foundation.
  • Treat you as a fragile oddity, assuming you're unable to perform simple tasks with your vestigial anatomy.

Physical Description

Through untold generations of selective and psychically guided evolution, contemplatives have transformed from some long- forgotten species with humanlike proportions into immense brains with mere vestiges of their abdomens and limbs dangling underneath like the gondola of some cerebral blimp.

The immense brain is roughly 70 pounds of a typical contemplative's 100-pound weight. Hardened layers of skin and cartilage cover the exterior, giving the brain a tough yet extremely pliable texture. The huge brain isn't just for show; a contemplative can store and process vast amounts of information by running independent conscious and subconscious processes subdivided across its four quadrants. Two quadrants handle tasks like motor function, language, and reasoning, akin to a human brain's two hemispheres.

Though capable of conscious thought too, the other two quadrants primarily generate a contemplative's psychic fields. Buoyed by this telekinetic force, a contemplative learns to fly with precision, if not always with speed. Even more importantly, a contemplative's telekinetic field helps support the brain; the organ lacks the structure needed to maintain its shape in standard gravity without psychic support. If rendered truly unconscious, a contemplative risks suffocation, internal bleeding, and weeklong migraines if they survive. Fortunately, they instinctively rest portions of their brain at different times, much like aquatic mammals, in a process called logging.

By comparison, a contemplative's lower body seems puny. Tight skin covers its gaunt frame, with spiky bones showing where powerful muscles might have attached themselves millennia ago. A contemplative has two sets of forelimbs, akin to a shirren, with only the upper set capable of manipulating objects in any meaningful way. The lower set act more like antennae, parsing olfactory and tactile information. A contemplative often hovers over a curiosity to analyze it with an array of senses, including the eyes that jut out a short distance from beneath the brain.

Their legs have slightly more strength than their arms. If necessary, a contemplative can waddle short distances before tiring. More often, though, their legs act as stabilizers for pushing off surfaces and reorienting the body in midair.

Some contemplatives find the concept of having a vestigial body at all so distasteful or unnecessary that they wear garments designed to veil it completely, giving them the appearance of a ghostly brain trailing a billowing shroud beneath them.

Society

Contemplative society carefully straddles the competing objectives of isolation and connection. A contemplative thrives in quiet spaces where they're shielded from distractions so they can dive deep into thought. On Akiton, they carved out vast caverns and subterranean cities that didn't just block conventional noise; these places deflected cosmic rays, radiation, and other minute signals that could distract a contemplative's acute senses. Their brains are like great telescopes, working best when far away from the pollution of others' thoughts while they strain to hear the universe's psychic signatures. In practice, this preference for isolation gives many contemplatives an aloof, standoffish quality that discourages some would-be companions.

At the same time, contemplatives have evolved to crave community. The same psychic fields that buoy a contemplative's mass also radiate telepathic signals that act like mental pheromones. Contemplatives subconsciously network and compare notes when living together, borrowing each other's perspectives and mental processing power. This chatter reflexively communicates their biases and moods, which has trained most contemplatives to be straightforward, analytical, and (by most creatures' standards) brash when they openly psychoanalyze and second-guess others. For a contemplative, sugar-coating feedback risks muddling the message.

Though unflinching honesty and dispassionate feedback are deeply valued among contemplative communities, contemplatives who leave the forums of Akiton quickly catch onto and master societal norms, learning to tiptoe around, appreciate, or even exploit others' taboos. After all, chasing away potential colleagues would deny a contemplative the comforting hum of others' thoughts. Adventuring contemplatives enjoy company for another reason: personal drive. Each companion is a potential rival or measuring stick against which a contemplative can judge their own worth, fueling their drive to learn more and (inevitably) outclass those around them. Half the time, a companion isn't even aware that their contemplative friend is engaged in this personal quest for dominance and validation.

Sample Names

Eskari's Third Concerto, Bones Land in a Spiral 16:3, Pokolv's Last Koan, Drift's Gravitational Constant, Toshoo Verse 4, Sonnet on Desna's Wing, Castrovelian Symmetry Paradox, Battle of Purple Valkyries.

Beliefs

To a contemplative, understanding is a matter of experimentation, philosophizing, discovery, and hard work. By contrast, divine revelation is a grotesque exercise that needs stronger citations. As a result, contemplatives rarely acknowledge deities through worship. Instead, gods are treated like famous colleagues and living libraries in the greater cosmos important (and often biased) sources of information that can help a contemplative unravel even greater truths. Any god who demands obedience, commands pointless rituals, or makes unsubstantiated claims about reality earns few contemplative followers.

Contemplatives respect and might obliquely worship deities who encourage their followers to research cosmic answers for themselves. The likes of Eloritu and Yaraesa are natural fits. Their edicts encourage what most contemplatives were already doing, but some commune with these deities as a student might with their academic advisor, seeking guidance and occasional collaboration. Ibra, Oras, and the Newborn are more like divine puzzles whom some contemplatives simultaneously worship and endeavor to decipher; if those deities hold more unsolved mysteries, they're worthy of further praise and attention. Those contemplatives dedicated to specific fields such as sociology and technology gravitate toward gods like Talavet and Triune. Finally, gods of community like Hylax and Lambatuin sometimes earn contemplative priests; however, some of these followers try to establish a permanent telepathic rapport with their patron as if they were another contemplative, often resulting in a flood of oracular (and cursed) insight.

The esoteric concept of Ashok lies at the center of contemplative belief. Exactly what Ashok is varies by the individual and the historian anything from a philosophical system to a legendary seer in the past to a mystical state of enlightenment yet to be achieved. Whatever definition a contemplative adopts, the concept of Ashok is one of aspiration and reverence. It goads a contemplative out of complacency, urging them to keep learning, exploring, and striving. Truly mastering Ashok would be a presumptuous claim; it's an indefinite process, perhaps on par with becoming a god or transforming into a being of pure thought. It's almost as presumptuous for a non-contemplative to engage with Ashok, much less claim to understand it. Few outsiders earn the honor of being initiated in the Ashok mysteries.

Popular Edicts

collaborate with peers to overcome problems, embrace moments of solitude and silence, engage in deep self-reflection, excel in a specialized field, seek Ashok

Popular Anathema

be content with simple explanations to complex problems, corrupt groundbreaking research, make assertions without evidence, suffer ignorance, value feelings over facts

Contemplative Mechanics

Hit Points 6

Size Medium

Speed 5 Feet (Hover); Fly 20 Feet

Attribute Boosts Charisma, Intelligence, Free

Attribute Flaw Strength

Languages Akitonian, Common

Additional languages equal to your Intelligence modifier (if positive). Choose from the list of common languages and any other languages to which you have access (such as the languages prevalent on your home world).

Traits

  • aberration
  • contemplative

Senses Low-Light Vision

Thoughtsense

Dragonkin.name: 龙人 Dragonkin
Dragonkin.text:
最初的龙人诞生于巨龙与类人生物之间深厚的爱之联结，借助复杂仪式与仅见于崔阿克斯星球的特殊材料而生。自第一批龙人诞生至今，已过去数千年，而他们也早已与巨龙分离——他们拥有自身独立于遥远龙类祖先之外的文化传统、社群与血脉。尽管岁月流转，赋予他们生命的爱仍在伙伴联结中延续——这是一种深刻的精神连接，每一位龙人都能与另一人建立。

从表面看，伙伴联结是一种通过情感锻造而成的心灵感应连接。它使缔结联结的双方能够以心灵感应交流，并如同一体般行动，在战斗、运动以及无数其他领域中获得优势。更深层地说，伙伴联结只能通过相互的奉献与爱来形成——无论是浪漫之爱、柏拉图式之爱，还是亲情——而且几乎无法破坏。这不是一种可以轻率缔结的联结，也无法被强迫建立或被任何事物切断，唯有死亡或诸神的意志除外。尽管伙伴联结在战术上的应用极为广泛，但它永远无法“关闭”或被忽视，所有外部关系都必须为其让步——伙伴之间几乎不会将任何忠诚置于彼此之上。

龙人极具社交性。他们与生俱来的共情能力与直觉使他们同时显得谨慎而真诚；他们往往会小心地接近新的人与新的处境，迅速形成判断，然后相信自己内心的直觉，即使这可能与他人的看法相冲突，或引发纷争。龙人的行为通常会受到其责任感与义务感的调节。一个缺乏连接——或对他人漠不关心——的龙人，可能会造成极大的混乱。

如果你想扮演一个强壮而坚韧、拥抱自身情感，并建立深厚友谊与社群纽带的角色，你就应该扮演一名龙人。

你可能会……

  • 与身边的每个人分享你的感受。
  • 比起既定事实，更相信自己的直觉。
  • 将与你缔结联结的伙伴的需求与愿望置于优先地位。

他人可能会……

  • 对你感到畏惧。
  • 低估你与你的伙伴之间联结的力量。
  • 在你和你的伙伴行动得完美一致时感到不安。

外貌描述

正如其名所示，龙人拥有龙类外貌，具备宽阔的翅膀、坚硬的鳞片、修长的尾巴、角，以及强健有力的体魄。有些龙人甚至继承了其龙类先祖致命的吐息，但吐息的类型与特征取决于多种因素，其中许多仍未被充分理解。龙人的体型差异很大，从头到尾长度约为8至20英尺，体重可超过2,000磅，但在现代，较小的体型已被证明更具优势。

龙人的鳞片颜色与他们的起源、传承、母星或周遭环境毫无关系。大多数龙人的鳞片为单色，不过有些会呈现两到三种相近色调，通常表现为渐变或斑点图案。大多数龙人都会将鳞片打磨得光亮可鉴，并会对鳞片在一天中某些时刻或特定光照下的外观尤为自豪——其中以月光下最为常见。

龙人偶尔会用各种颜色与图案装饰自己的爪子和角，不过极少有人会改造或染色自己的鳞片——更不用说永久性的了！希望暂时改变鳞片外观的龙人，更偏好使用全息覆层、增强现实或魔法幻象。这一趋势中一个显著的例外是 dolek（读作 doe-wek），这是一种传统仪式：从自己身上取下一片鳞片，将其制成可佩戴的礼物赠予自己的联结伙伴。在遥远的过去，被取下的鳞片来自背部——因为那是联结伙伴若骑乘时会坐在其背上的位置。如今，更常见的是取下心脏上方的一片鳞。无论是哪一种情况，dolek 之礼都象征着龙人信任其伙伴会保护自己的弱点——无论是身体上的、心理上的，还是情感上的。

社群

龙人的历史、社群与文化，与他们的母星崔阿克斯的历史密不可分；自远古以来，他们便与雷弗瑞亚人和巨龙一同生活于此。崔阿克斯拥有夸张的椭圆轨道，这导致其冬夏都漫长而严酷，可持续数个世纪，二者之间仅有短暂的过渡季节。无论是生理上的还是行为上的适应，一直都是生存的关键组成部分，即使在今天，龙人仍极其重视适应能力、在艰难处境中坚持下去的意愿，以及在自然诸多考验下存活的能力。人们用来实现这些目标的方法——从体能上的强悍、精神上的机敏，到魔法支援或电控机械植入体——都会普遍受到赞誉。

龙人有着好战的历史。早在空白期之前，崔阿克斯各族之间的战争便十分普遍，其动因包括领土争端与延续了无数纪元的龙族宿怨。在那个遥远的时代，龙人战士会驮载雷弗瑞亚骑手，与强大的巨龙盟友一起编队冲入战场。这些战斗搭档是所有伙伴联结中最典型、也最具代表性的形式，而这一传统至今仍然延续。与其他族裔相比，龙人至今仍最有可能与雷弗瑞亚人结为伙伴，而现代最受尊敬的佣兵联盟之一便是航行太空的天火军团。尽管龙人在社群中承担着无数不同角色，但在更广阔的银河里，他们仍最为人所知的是其令人生畏的外表与军事专长。即便是那些避开冲突或厌恶暴力的龙人，也会在危险来临之时学会保护自己——或自己在意的人。

龙人社群建立在伙伴关系之上。从微观层面来说，伙伴联结是日常生活的锚点；而从宏观层面来说，一切都围绕社群运转。龙人创造的一切，从建筑与基础设施到法律与媒体，都反映出伙伴关系的重要性。

示例名字

Akrodo, Aralyx, Axiennex, Beshikasiss, Binata, Darzalfen, Dissidanth, Egelmyr, Endrisarn, Estrikkon, Farvenzi, Ganifrae, Heraxia, Kahore, Krellenor, Lourvictrix, Phixara, Raskurenn, Selkirrik, Sozirenn, Tarke, Tsalu, Tykkendrin, Zendegos, Ziotris

信念

龙人极为重视人际关系与社交能力，但他们也明白，一个人是否迷人、是否是个好朋友或好伙伴，与其道德品质和伦理立场并无必然关系。他们天生强健，而大多数龙人都会通过学习自卫或主宰战场来拥抱祖先的传承。出于在严酷而危险的母星环境中生存的需要，龙人会利用一切可用手段努力适应、求生并坚持下去。大多数龙人崇拜那些能体现这些价值之一的神祇，例如戴莫力图斯、Eloritu、Hylax、Oras、塔拉维特，或达戈萨恩——这位崔阿克斯的社群、伙伴关系与联结之神。

常见训令

保护你的伙伴，以积极方式参与自己的社群，为守护你的信念与盟友而战

常见禁忌

怯懦行事，背叛你的伙伴，故意孤立自己

龙人机制

生命值 10

体型 大型

速度 20尺；飞行 20尺

属性加值 两项自由属性加值

语言 通用语、龙语、崔阿克斯语

额外语言 额外语言数量等于你的智力调整值（若为正值）。从常见语言列表中选择，并可选择任何其他你能够接触到的语言（例如你母星上流行的语言）。

特征

  • dragon
  • dragonkin
  • humanoid

感官 黑暗视觉

伙伴联结

The first dragonkin were born from deep bonds of love between dragons and humanoids using complex rituals and special materials found only on the planet Triaxus. It's been millennia since the first dragonkin were born, and they have long since disconnected from dragons—they have their own cultural traditions, societies, and lineages, independent of their distant draconic forebears. Despite the ages, the love that gave birth to them endures in the partner bond—a deep spiritual connection that every dragonkin can forge with one other person.

On the surface, a partner bond is a telepathic connection forged through emotion. It enables a bonded pair to communicate telepathically and react as one, providing advantages in battle, sport, and countless other avenues. Looking deeper, a partner bond can only be formed through mutual devotion and love—whether romantic, platonic, or familial—and is nigh unbreakable. It's not a bond to be entered into frivolously, nor can it be forced or severed by anything other than death or the will of the gods. While the tactical applications of a partner bond are extensive, it can never be “turned off” or ignored, and all outside relationships must bend to accommodate it—rarely is there a loyalty that partners place above one another.

Dragonkin are extremely social. Their innate empathy and intuition makes them simultaneously cautious and earnest; they tend to approach new people and situations with care, form an opinion rapidly, then trust their own gut judgment, even if it might conflict with the opinions of others or cause strife. A dragonkin's behavior is usually tempered by their sense of duty and responsibility. A dragonkin with few connections—or who shows little regard for others—can cause a lot of havoc.

If you want to play a character who is strong and resilient, embraces their emotions, and forms deep bonds of friendship and community, you should play a dragonkin.

You Might...

  • Share your feelings with everyone around you.
  • Trust your instincts more than you trust established facts.
  • Prioritize your bonded partner's needs and wants.

Others Probably...

  • Are intimidated by you.
  • Underestimate the strength of your bond with your partner.
  • Find it disconcerting when you and your partner act in perfect unison.

Physical Description

As their name implies, dragonkin are draconic in appearance, with broad wings, hard scales, long tails, horns, and a powerful physique. Some even have the deadly breath of their dragon progenitors, but the breath's type and characteristics depend on a number of factors, many of which aren't well understood. Dragonkin vary between 8–20 feet long head to tail and can weigh over 2,000 pounds, but in the modern era a smaller size has proven advantageous.

A dragonkin's scale color has nothing to do with their origins, heritage, home world, or surroundings. Most are monochromatic, though some have scales in two or three similar tones, most often in a gradient or speckled pattern. Most dragonkin polish their scales to a reflective shine and take particular pride in the appearance of their scales at certain times of the day or under certain lighting— under moonlight is most common.

Dragonkin occasionally paint their claws and horns with various colors and patterns, though very few modify or dye their scales—certainly not permanently! Dragonkin looking to temporarily change the appearance of their scales prefer holographic overlays, augmented reality, or magical illusions. The notable exception to this trend involves dolek (pronounced doe-wek), the traditional ritual of removing a single scale from one's body and fashioning it into a wearable gift for one's bonded partner. In the distant past, the scale removed came from the back—the place that a bonded partner would sit upon one's back if ridden. Today, it's more common to remove a scale from above the heart. In either case, the gift of a dolek is a symbol that a dragonkin trusts their partner to protect their weak spots, physically, psychologically, and emotionally.

Society

The history, society, and culture of dragonkin is inexorably linked with that of their home world, Triaxus, where they've lived alongside ryphorians and dragons since time immemorial. Triaxus has an exaggerated elliptical orbit that causes grueling, century-long winters and summers, with only a short transitional season between them. Adaptation, whether biological or behavioral, has always been integral to survival, and even today dragonkin greatly value adaptability, willingness to persevere through trying situations, and ability to survive nature's many trials. The methods one uses to accomplish these feats—from physical prowess or mental cunning to magical support or cybernetic augmentations—are universally lauded.

Dragonkin have a combative history. Long before the Gap, battles between Triaxus's peoples were widespread, motivated by territorial disputes and aeons-old draconic rivalries. In this distant era, dragonkin warriors carried ryphorian riders into battle in formation with powerful dragon allies. These battle pairings were the typical and most iconic of all partner bonds, and their legacy still lives on today. Dragonkin remain most likely to partner with ryphorians over other ancestries, and one of the most respected mercenary leagues of the modern era is the spacefaring Skyfire Legion. Although dragonkin fulfill countless roles in society, they remain best known in the greater galaxy for their intimidating appearance and military expertise. Even those dragonkin who avoid confrontation or abhor violence learn to defend themselves—or the people they care about—in times of peril.

Dragonkin society is based around partnership. On a micro scale, the partner bond anchors daily life, but on a macro scale, everything revolves around community. Everything dragonkin create, from architecture and infrastructure to laws and media, reflects the importance of partnership.

Sample Names

Akrodo, Aralyx, Axiennex, Beshikasiss, Binata, Darzalfen, Dissidanth, Egelmyr, Endrisarn, Estrikkon, Farvenzi, Ganifrae, Heraxia, Kahore, Krellenor, Lourvictrix, Phixara, Raskurenn, Selkirrik, Sozirenn, Tarke, Tsalu, Tykkendrin, Zendegos, Ziotris

Beliefs

Dragonkin place great value on interpersonal relationships and social skills, yet they recognize that the ability to be charming, or a good friend or partner, is independent of one's moral standing and ethical stance. They're naturally strong, and most embrace the legacy of their ancestors by learning to defend themselves or to dominate the field of battle. Driven by the need to survive the harsh and dangerous environment of their home world, dragonkin strive to adapt, survive, and persevere using any means available. Most dragonkin worship gods that speak to one of these values, such as Damoritosh, Eloritu, Hylax, Oras, Talavet, or Dagosarn, the Triaxian god of community, partnerships, and the bond.

Popular Edicts

protect your partner, engage positively with your community, fight to defend your beliefs and allies

Popular Anathema

act with cowardice, betray your partner, purposefully isolate yourself

Dragonkin Mechanics

Hit Points 10

Size Large

Speed 20 Feet; Fly 20 Feet

Attribute Boosts Two free attribute boosts

Languages Common, Draconic, Triaxian

Additional languages Additional languages equal to your Intelligence modifier (if positive). Choose from the list of common languages and any other languages to which you have access (such as the languages prevalent on your home world).

Traits

  • dragon
  • dragonkin
  • humanoid

Senses Darkvision

Partner Bond

Guilt of the Graveworld.name: 墓界的负罪感 Guilt of the Graveworld

Elebrian.name: 灵脑人 Elebrian
Elebrian.text:
灵脑人是曾经以厄克斯星球为家园的显赫物种仅存的活体后裔。在那场可怕而神秘的大灾变发生之前，厄克斯原本是他们的故乡；那场灾变烧尽了大气层，并将这颗星球上鲜活的动植物焚烧殆尽。不死生物死灵妖——也就是那些曾通过魔法仪式改造自己、以便在毁灭的世界上存活下来的灵脑人——统治着厄克斯，而一小群活着的灵脑人则被困在埋藏于德米纳斯洼地之下的一处设施中。或许还有其他灵脑人群体在厄克斯各地孤立的掩体中幸存下来，但如今，人口集中于生者大厅——一座地下媒体综合体，活人们在那里为不死观众提供娱乐。

灵脑人天生拥有对魔法的第六感。由于他们脑中存在特殊结构，导致头部增大，灵脑人能够切实感受到魔法能量。那些学会磨炼这种特殊感知的灵脑人，往往会成为强大的贤者，能够驾驭元素、剖析奥术理论，甚至改写现实。

灵脑人通常五官匀称，但颅骨 enlarged、额头高耸，肤色从苍白到深棕不等。大多数现代灵脑人并不觉得自己与其物种几近灭绝之前曾经昌盛的古老文化有多少联系。现代灵脑人文化并非建立在族群或宗教之上——而是建立在商业媒体之上。只有极少数人在幼年时随家人离开生者大厅，或在极其罕见的外来者社群中长大，才可能按照他人的标准宣称自己过过“正常”生活。

如果你想扮演一个富有戏剧感、背景离奇地受到保护，并且第一次体验更广阔银河的角色，你应该考虑扮演灵脑人。

你可能会……

  • 不惜一切代价活下去。
  • 以叛逆的姿态寻求让你的物种摆脱他们在厄克斯所谓的“生活”。
  • 认为自己比别人更懂娱乐。

其他人可能会……

  • 认为你和厄克斯的不死生物死灵妖，甚至像尸骸舰队这样的团体有所关联。
  • 怀疑你展现的是真正的自我，还是只是在扮演另一个角色。
  • 从你过去的媒体露面中认出你。

外貌描述

灵脑人最显著的身体特征，是其为容纳增大的大脑及专门的奥术感知器官而进化出的巨大颅骨和隆起额头。自颈部以下看，灵脑人与人类几乎难以区分，这也让一些边缘理论得以延续：他们或许与人类拥有共同的演化祖先。大多数成年灵脑人身高在5到6英尺之间，肤色从桃色到小麦色不等，眼睛多为深黑、蓝色或棕色。灵脑人通常完全无毛，只有极少数个体能长出细眉、胡须，甚至是几代人才会出现一次的山羊胡。有些灵脑人会在光头上佩戴华丽头饰，或用精致纹身覆盖头顶；与死灵妖同类相比，他们往往偏爱更鲜艳的颜色。

社群

总体而言，灵脑人坚韧而沉着，接受自己所得到的一切，并努力将其发挥到最好。现代灵脑人文化与灾难性的“死亡哀号”之前的样貌已相去甚远，而厄克斯主流的灵脑人社群，则围绕对名声的追逐，以及娱乐内容的创作与消费而运转。

在生者大厅长大的灵脑人，从幼年起就习惯了镜头与为观众表演，尽管在法律上，他们在成年之前不得出现在丧媒体的节目中。丧媒体仍持续为想要“出国留学”的年轻灵脑人提供“奖学金”——这是让他们迁往其他星球、在那里过上远离镜头生活的委婉说法。

示例姓名

Ahmzovar, Auradorus, Elyriana, Eterrina, Fenthest, Genarathis, Missavuine, Sadrat, Sanimus, Scaparitos, Shathrava, Telmarcius, Thesteline, Veltheniel

信念

许多灵脑人过着一种离奇而受保护的生活，但居住在生者大厅的人会被来自整个银河的媒体内容不断轰炸，使他们接触到大量新观念。有些灵脑人会亲自挑选属于自己的混合式信仰，或采纳另一种文化的传统。对于崇拜神祇的灵脑人来说，厄洛里图与兰巴图因是颇受欢迎的选择。灵脑人社群倾向于无神论。任何祖先神灵早已被遗忘或抛弃，取而代之的是银幕霓虹之神。厄克斯上的许多灵脑人都遵循“寂静之歌”，这是一种哲学，认为凡人的一生不过是为第二次、也就是不死存在所做的一次试演。最终，当面对衰老、长期疾病或死亡的前景时，大多数灵脑人都会寻求转化为不死生物。自然死亡的灵脑人十分罕见，而做出这种选择在他们厄克斯同胞眼中也会被视为反常。

常见典范

活出值得被铭记的人生，演好你的角色，追求名望

常见禁忌

任由自己渐渐消逝

灵脑人机制

生命值 6

体型 中型

速度 25英尺

属性增强 体质、智力、自由

属性缺陷 感知

语言 通用语、厄克斯语

额外语言 等于你的智力调整值（如果其为正值）。从常见语言列表以及你可接触到的任何其他语言中选择（例如你母星上流行的语言）。

特征

  • 灵脑人
  • 类人生物

魔法感知

@Embed[Compendium.sf2e.ancestry-features.Item.6Br8G6BBZ0xWw7Pj inline]

灵脑人传承

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.heritages.Item.A3XpAjUgnGyxXoDI]{演艺者灵脑人}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.heritages.Item.UjSJHYe9RRXbGHl2]{流亡者灵脑人}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.heritages.Item.EXwIrrzLZQZODLOd]{贤者生灵脑人}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.heritages.Item.llkEXzVjzZVl9lHW]{舞台助手灵脑人}

Elebrians are the last living descendants of the prominent species that once called the planet Eox home, prior to a terrible and mysterious cataclysm that burned away its atmosphere and immolated the planet's living flora and fauna. The undead necrovites—once elebrians who transformed themselves with magical ritual to survive on their ruined world—ruled Eox while a small community of living elebrians remained trapped in a facility buried beneath Deminas Hollow. Other groups of elebrians might have survived in isolated bunkers across Eox, but today the population is concentrated in the Halls of the Living, an underground media complex where the living provide entertainment for an undead audience.

Elebrians are born with a sixth sense for magic. Thanks to special structures in their brains that cause their enlarged heads, elebrians literally feel magical energies. Elebrians who learn to hone their special sense often become powerful sages who command the elements, parse arcane theory, or even rewrite reality.

Elebrians tend to have even features with enlarged craniums, high foreheads, and skin tones that vary from pale to deep umber. Most modern elebrians don't feel connected to ancient cultures that were prominent before the near extinction of their species. Modern elebrian culture isn't based on ethnicity or religion—it's based on commercial media. Only the few elebrians whose families left the Halls of the Living when they were children, or who were raised in extremely rare outsider communities, could claim to have led a “normal” life by anyone else's standards.

If you want to play a character with a flair for the dramatic and a strangely sheltered background who's experiencing the wider galaxy for the first time, you should consider playing an elebrian.

You Might...

  • Do whatever it takes to survive.
  • Rebelliously seek to free your species from their so-called “lives” on Eox.
  • Think you know more about entertainment than others do.

Others Probably...

  • Assume you have connections with the undead necrovites of Eox or even groups like the Corpse Fleet.
  • Wonder if you're being your true self or just playing another role.
  • Recognize you from past media appearances.

Physical Description

The most notable physical feature of elebrians is their enlarged cranium and distended forehead, evolved to house their enlarged brains possessing specialized arcane sensory organs. From the neck down, elebrians look nearly indistinguishable from a human, keeping alive fringe theories about them sharing evolutionary ancestors with humans. Most mature elebrians are 5 to 6 feet tall, with a range of peach to tan skin tones and mostly dark black, blue, or brown eyes. Elebrians are usually completely hairless, except for a rare few individuals who manage to grow thin eyebrows, mustaches, or even a once-in-a-generation goatee. Some elebrians wear opulent headdresses on their bald heads or cover their crowns with elaborate tattoos, tending to favor brighter colors than their necrovite counterparts.

Society

In general, elebrians are hearty and stoic, accepting what they're given and striving to make the best of it. Modern elebrian cultures have drifted far from what existed before the calamitous Death Wail, and mainstream elebrian society on Eox is centered around the pursuit of fame and creating or consuming entertainment.

Elebrians who grow up in the Halls of the Living become accustomed to cameras and performing for an audience from early childhood, though they legally aren't allowed to appear on Zo! Media programming before reaching adulthood. Zo! Media continues sponsoring “scholarships” for young elebrians who want to “study abroad,” code for relocation to a planet where they can live their life off- screen.

Sample Names

Ahmzovar, Auradorus, Elyriana, Eterrina, Fenthest, Genarathis, Missavuine, Sadrat, Sanimus, Scaparitos, Shathrava, Telmarcius, Thesteline, Veltheniel

Beliefs

Many elebrians lead strangely sheltered lives, but those residing in the Halls of the Living are bombarded with media from all over the galaxy, exposing them to a multitude of new ideas. Some elebrians handpick their own eclectic beliefs or adopt the traditions of another culture. Eloritu and Lambatuin are popular choices for elebrians who worship deities. Elebrian society tends to be atheistic. Any ancestral gods have long been forgotten or abandoned in favor of the neon gods of the silver screen. Many elebrians on Eox follow the Song of Silence, a philosophy that teaches that a being's mortal life is simply a trial run for one's second, undead existence. Eventually, most elebrians seek to transition into undeath when faced with the prospect of aging, long-term illness, or death. Elebrians who die naturally are rare, and the choice to do so is perceived as unusual by their Eoxian cohorts.

Popular Edicts

live a life worth remembering, play your part, seek fame

Popular Anathema

allow yourself to fade away

Elebrian Mechanics

Hit Points 6

Size Medium

Speed 25 Feet

Attribute Boosts Constitution, Intelligence, Free

Attribute Flaw Wisdom

Languages Common, Eoxian

Additional languages equal to your Intelligence modifier (if it's positive). Choose from the list of common languages and any other languages to which you have access (such as the languages prevalent on your home world).

Traits

  • elebrian
  • humanoid

Magical Sense

Invasion's Edge Player's Guide.name: 《入侵边缘》玩家指南 Invasion's Edge Player's Guide

Ikeshti.name: 异客蜥人 Ikeshti
Ikeshti.text:
异客蜥人受其环境塑造——阿基顿星球上严酷的沙漠、丘陵与荒地；这是一个资源与机遇都日渐枯竭的世界。他们坚韧顽强，具有强大的耐受力，即使一周多不进食不饮水也能存活。每一代都会形成各自紧密相连的社群，而且形式差异极大：从游牧部族、务农宅地，到流动的机车帮派、商业帝国，以及介于其间的一切类型。大多数离开自己社群的异客蜥人都会向家乡回送支援，通常以资源、情报或信用点的形式。

由于长期受匮乏环境塑造，异客蜥人痛恨浪费，几乎从不认为任何东西毫无价值——不能再用的东西也能修理、改作他用，或拆成零件制成新物。许多异客蜥人以自己的节俭为荣，并在某种实用工艺或手艺上磨炼出精湛技能。和许多阿基顿居民一样，异客蜥人把每一处垃圾场、废料堆和被遗忘的残骸都视为充满希望的机会。他们以聪明才智与巧思闻名，许多异客蜥人社群以拾荒者、废料商人与机械师为生。

异客蜥人情感强烈、热情奔放，而压力与危险会进一步放大他们的情绪。许多异客蜥人难以用健康的方式处理情绪，因此现代异客蜥人会学习去面对并接纳——而非忽视——自己的情感，建立有助于减压与自我稳定的日常习惯与行为，并向可信赖的朋友倾诉。大多数异客蜥人情绪多变，常有剧烈的心境波动。当情绪不堪重负或感到挫败时，他们容易爆发出悲伤或攻击性。这也让异客蜥人落下了性急、喜怒无常且容易突然诉诸暴力的名声。

年幼的异客蜥人，以及那些未能培养出足以处理自身情绪之成熟度的成年个体，在压力或挫败下还会有陷入令人畏惧的“撕裂者”状态的风险。这种突如其来的变化会使身体肿胀膨大，让异客蜥人变得更高大、更强壮，但他们的心智会被愤怒彻底压倒。处于撕裂者状态的异客蜥人会狂暴肆虐，摧毁路径上的一切人与物，往往连自己的社群与亲族也会受害。这种变化持续时间不长，却极其耗竭体力，通常会持续到该异客蜥人冷静下来或耗尽能量为止。异客蜥人认为撕裂者状态极其丢脸——就像在公众场合语无伦次地大发脾气一样。有些异客蜥人能够主动进入撕裂者状态，并在转化期间维持自己的理智，以在绝境中获取力量。讽刺的是，掌控撕裂者变形需要极端强大的情绪控制力，而这样的异客蜥人也因此备受尊敬。

如果你想扮演一名富有创造力且充满热情的幸存者，重视自己的社群，并且在面对复杂局面与艰难困苦时依然坚定不移，那么你就应该扮演异客蜥人。

你可能会……

  • 乐于为旧物发掘新用途，并赋予损坏之物新的生命。
  • 对自己的社群及其成员怀有深厚连接，并努力确保他们持续生存或繁荣昌盛。
  • 经历情绪波动，或经常感到情绪难以承受。
  • 善于自省并重视心理健康，可能拥有喜爱的爱好、偏好的日常惯例、可信赖的倾诉对象，或一套你会用来减压的固定行为。

其他人可能会……

  • 对你的聪明才智印象深刻。
  • 认为你喜爱旧物和拾来的东西，说明你手头拮据。
  • 觉得你脾气反复无常、情绪化又夸张。
  • 担心要是惹怒你，你就会变成怪物。

外貌描述

异客蜥人是双足行走的爬行动物，拥有长尾、带爪的双手以及细长的吻部。他们体型小巧袖珍，平均身高约在2-1/2至3-1/2英尺之间。他们坚硬的鳞片呈红色，有时会带有棕色、橙色或粉色的渐变。多数异客蜥人在头部后方与颈部背面长有褶边，肘部或膝部长有骨刺，并具有锐利牙齿和黄色眼睛。有些异客蜥人会在头部两侧长出向下弯曲的角，不过这种情况最常见于年长的异客蜥人，以及那些经常进入撕裂者状态者。

社群

异客蜥人在一生中始终由自己的社群所定义，而这一切始于“育群”。育群是由许多窝卵孵化出的幼体组成的贪食群体，由育群照料者抚养——这些异客蜥人专门负责养育和教育幼年个体。刚孵化的异客蜥人幼体除了长着尾巴、只会张口吞食之外几乎什么都不是；随着成长，他们会逐渐长出更多肢体。他们会本能地寻求彼此陪伴，形成四处游荡的群体，机械般地吞食有机物。育群很容易摧毁一片地区的动植物，或横扫一个聚居地，因此育群照料者会确保他们的育群以负责任的方式进食。这些努力既保护了当地生态系统，也让育群免受捕食者与愤怒居民的威胁。

随着幼体逐渐成熟，育群照料者会开始更正式的教育，教授说话、负责任地进食、情绪觉察，以及撕裂者状态的危险等重要生活技能。通过实际行动，异客蜥人学会个人责任、社群繁荣与合作。

五年后，异客蜥人会脱离自己的育群，组建各自独特的群体。许多这样的新生社群起初是游牧流浪者、务农农夫、巧手拾荒者或投机帮派，不过随着时间推移，他们会在积累经验或产生创新后改变并适应自己的生活方式。各个社群彼此缔结联盟，以获取保护、陪伴与贸易机会，从而形成一张复杂的关系与同盟之网，使这些社群得以作为更广大整体的一部分而繁荣。有时，多个社群会合并成更大的单位，但绝不会扩张到无法维持居住的地步，总是保持在其环境能够安全承载的阈值之内。

由于异客蜥人社群在形式与功能上都十分多样，因此几乎没有什么是绝对共通的。每个社群都会找到一种在经济繁荣与社会满足之间取得平衡的方法，通常会将其聪明才智与机械技能发挥得淋漓尽致。战士、猎手、斥候与拾荒者都很重要，治疗者和社群领袖亦然。抚魂者（治疗师）与育群照料者通常是社群中最受尊崇的角色。

异客蜥人很少对繁衍后代表现出兴趣，即便有，也鲜少真正付诸实施，因为他们清楚一个育群会给其所在区域带来多大负担。异客蜥人会在远离自身社群的地方产下大量卵，并由育群照料者照看。由于引导一个育群会让异客蜥人多年无法回到自己的社群，因此只有最有耐心、最懂照料、最富牺牲精神的异客蜥人才会承担这一职责。育群照料者会得到其最亲近伙伴的支持——通常是其他育群照料者、朋友与爱侣。在任期内，育群照料者会非常保护自己的育群，也因此常显得脾气暴躁、拒人于外。当他们的育群开始组成社群并离开巢地时，育群照料者便会满怀自豪地返回自己的社群。

在阿基顿求生并不容易，因此每个社群都会有成员选择离开家园（通常甚至离开整颗星球）去寻找新机会。大多数人会把钱、情报或补给送回家乡，从远方资助自己的社群。许多人会成为矿工、工程师、机械师、创业者或角斗士，或投身娱乐业，在音乐或体育领域追逐名声与财富。另一些人则有意识地外出获取货物、贸易协议、资产与资金，以便直接输送给自己的社群。许多人加入像阿巴达尔集团这样的合作，创办商业事业，并进行股票交易。无论采用何种方法或追求何种目标，那些会把资金寄回家乡的异客蜥人都被称为“会众”。

归航者残骸的到来吸引了许多社群与无数会众的注意，他们都渴望探索这艘残骸、发现新技术，并将有趣的打捞物改作他用。许多人已经定居在繁荣新镇“窃者天堂”，或与领导前往残骸远征的各派系合作，其中包括寻星者协会。

异客蜥人的命名惯例因社群而异，但通常偏好多摩擦音与爆破辅音。

示例姓名

Asashla, Bishae, Dezall, Geffi, Friyen, Ifray, Kish, Merthinett, Nenvi, Pavail, Quesch, Sehvash, Throsh, Tikesh, Vadak, Veke, Vertassh, Voss

信仰

传统上，异客蜥人很少关注有组织的宗教，而更重视对同育群者与自身社群的奉献。如今，他们崇拜形形色色的神祇，几乎没有什么会被视为异样或陌生。许多人崇拜能够反映其社群生活方式的神祇，例如海莱克斯或塔拉维特。游牧群体会与维丹产生亲近感，而更偏尚武的一支则偏爱戴莫力图斯或贝斯玛拉。会众往往更偏好阿巴达尔或兰巴图因。

极少数情况下，异客蜥人会成为狂热的先知，从未明力量那里获得幻象与智慧。这些预兆的来源仍是谜团，尽管他们鳞片上自然浮现的印记似乎暗示艾洛里图，或刚刚孵化的新诞者可能涉入其中；又或者，这一切源于存在边缘的污秽之物，或群星之间黑暗地带中的某种邪恶存在。

常见训令

尊重自己与他人的心理健康，保护你的社群，重复使用并改造物品与材料，保持节俭

常见禁忌

压抑或无视自己的情绪，背叛亲近之人，肆意造成破坏，浪费无度

异客蜥人机制

生命值 6

体型 小型

速度 25英尺；攀爬 15英尺

属性增强 敏捷、智力、自由

属性缺陷 感知

语言 阿基顿语、通用语，以及一种你自选的地区语言。

额外语言 数量等于你的智力调整值（如果其为正值）。从常见语言列表以及任何你有机会接触的其他语言中选择（例如你母星上通行的语言）。

特征

  • humanoid, ikeshti

荒地幸存者

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.heritages.Item.ezPp5n1TQKjhkZsy]{实验型异客蜥人}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.heritages.Item.Nkll4kC0WQW67wE8]{耐受异客蜥人}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.heritages.Item.JuqBWWB16ekM0Tdf]{娇养异客蜥人}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.heritages.Item.Vf8NOK1Jnk0zQDqv]{棘刺异客蜥人}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.heritages.Item.TwU15npTtpW9TJcv]{地下异客蜥人}

Ikeshtis are conditioned by their environment—the harsh deserts, hills, and badlands of the planet Akiton, a dwindling world low in resources and opportunities. They're hardy and resilient, capable of surviving over a week without food or water. Each generation forms its own tight-knit communities, which vary dramatically, from nomadic tribes and agrarian homesteads to roving biker gangs, mercantile empires, and everything in between. Most ikeshtis who leave their community send support back home, usually in the form of resources, information, or credits.

Shaped by scarcity, ikeshtis loathe wastefulness and rarely consider anything worthless—what can't be reused can be repaired, repurposed, or broken down for parts and crafted into something new. Many ikeshtis take pride in their thriftiness and have honed their skills with a tangible craft or trade. Like many inhabitants of Akiton, ikeshtis view every junkyard, scrapheap, and forgotten wreck as a promising opportunity. They're well known for their ingenuity and craftiness, and many ikeshti communities make their living as salvagers, scrap traders, and mechanics.

Ikeshtis are passionate and experience intense emotions, which are magnified by stress and danger. Many ikeshtis have difficulty processing their emotions in a healthy way, so modern ikeshtis learn to confront and embrace—not ignore—their emotions, develop routines and behaviors to help them de-stress or center themselves, and confide in trusted friends. Most ikeshtis are mercurial and experience frequent mood swings. When emotionally overwhelmed or frustrated, they're prone to outbursts of sadness or aggression. This has given ikeshtis a reputation for being hotheaded, temperamental, and prone to sudden violence.

Juvenile ikeshtis and adults who fail to develop the maturity necessary to process their emotions, additionally risk entering the dreaded rivener state when stressed or frustrated. This sudden transformation engorges the body, making the ikeshti bigger and stronger, but completely overrides their mind with anger. A rivener violently rampages, destroying everything and everyone in their path, often harming their own communities and kin. This transformation is short-lived and exhausting, lasting until the ikeshti calms or runs out of energy. Ikeshtis consider the rivener state incredibly embarrassing—like having an incoherent temper tantrum in public. Some ikeshtis can enter the rivener state intentionally, maintaining their mental faculties during the transformation, to gain strength in a desperate situation. Ironically, mastering the rivener transformation requires extreme emotional control, and such ikeshtis are highly respected.

If you want to play a creative and passionate survivor, who values their community and is determined in the face of complications and hardship, you should play an ikeshti.

You Might...

  • Enjoy finding new purposes for old things and giving broken things new life.
  • Feel a deep connection to your community and its members and strive to ensure their continued survival or prosperity.
  • Experience mood swings, or frequently feel emotionally overwhelmed.
  • Be introspective and invest in your mental health, likely having favorite hobbies, preferred routines, a trusted confidante, or practiced behaviors you engage in to de-stress.

Others Probably...

  • Are impressed by your ingenuity.
  • Assume your fondness for used and found objects indicates a lack of financial resources.
  • Consider you temperamental and melodramatic.
  • Worry you'll transform into a monster if they make you angry.

Physical Description

Ikeshtis are bipedal reptiles with a long tail, taloned hands, and an elongated snout. They're small and compact, averaging between 2-1/2 to 3-1/2 feet tall. Their hard scales are red, sometimes with brown, orange, or pink gradations. Most have frills running down the back of their head and neck, with spurs on the elbows or knees, sharp teeth, and yellow eyes. Some ikeshtis grow downward-curving horns on the side of their head, though this is most common in elder ikeshtis and those who frequently enter the rivener state.

Society

Ikeshtis are defined by their communities throughout their lives, beginning with the brood. A brood is a voracious swarm of infants hatched from numerous egg clutches and raised by brood-minders—ikeshtis who specialize in nurturing and educating juveniles. Ikeshti hatchlings are little more than gaping mouths with tails, driven by hunger, who sprout additional appendages as they age. They instinctively seek one another's company, forming roving groups that mindlessly consume organic matter. Broods can easily decimate a region's flora and fauna or tear through a settlement, so brood-minders ensure their broods eat responsibly. These efforts protect local ecosystems and keep broods safe from predators and angry locals.

As hatchlings mature, brood-minders begin a more formal education, teaching important life skills such as speech, responsible feeding, emotional awareness, and the dangers of the rivener state. Through action, ikeshtis learn personal responsibility, communal prosperity, and collaboration.

After five years, ikeshtis outgrow their brood and form their own unique groups. Many of these fledgling communities begin as nomadic wanderers, agrarian farmers, crafty salvagers, or opportunistic gangs, though they change with time, adapting their lifestyle as they gain experience or innovate. Communities form alliances with one another for protection, companionship, and trade, forming a complex web of relations and alliances that enable communities to thrive as part of a wider whole. Sometimes communities merge into larger units, but never become untenantable, always remaining within the threshold of what their environment can safely support.

As ikeshti communities are diverse in form and function, there are few universals. Each finds a method to balance economic prosperity with social satisfaction, usually leveraging their ingenuity and mechanical skills to great effect. Warriors, hunters, scouts, and salvagers are all important, as are healers and community leaders. Soul- soothers (therapists) and brood-minders are typically the most honored roles in a community.

Ikeshtis rarely show interest in procreation and, when they do, few follow through, mindful of the burden a brood will place on their region. Ikeshtis lay large clutches of eggs away from their communities, which are tended by brood-minders. As guiding a brood takes an ikeshti away from their own community for years at a time, only the most patient, nurturing, and self-sacrificing ikeshtis take on the role. Brood-minders are supported by their closest companions—usually other brood-minders, friends, and lovers. For their tenure, brood-minders are very protective of their brood, becoming surly and standoffish. When their brood begins to form communities and drift away from the nest, brood-minders return to their own communities with pride.

Surviving on Akiton is hard, so every community has members who choose to leave home (often the planet entirely) for new opportunities. Most send money, information, or supplies back home, remotely funding their community. Many find work as miners, engineers, mechanics, entrepreneurs, or gladiators, or enter the entertainment industry, chasing fame and fortune in music or sports. Others set out intentionally to acquire goods, trade deals, assets, and funds that can be directly funneled to their communities. Many join corporations such as AbadarCorp, form business ventures, and trade in stocks. Regardless of methods or goals, ikeshtis who send funds home are called congregants.

The arrival of the Wreck of the Returned drew the attention of many communities and countless congregants, all eager to explore the wreck, discover new technology, and repurpose interesting salvage. Many have settled in the boomtown of Pilferer's Paradise or teamed up with factions leading expeditions to the wreck, including the Starfinder Society.

Ikeshti naming conventions vary by community, but often favor fricative sounds and plosive consonants.

Sample Names

Asashla, Bishae, Dezall, Geffi, Friyen, Ifray, Kish, Merthinett, Nenvi, Pavail, Quesch, Sehvash, Throsh, Tikesh, Vadak, Veke, Vertassh, Voss

Beliefs

Traditionally, ikeshtis give little thought to organized religion, favoring devotion to one's broodmates and community. Today, they worship a diverse array of gods, with nothing considered foreign or strange. Many worship gods that reflect their communal lifestyles, such as Hylax or Talavet. Nomadic groups feel kinship with Weydan, and those of a more warlike bent favor Damoritosh or Besmara. Congregants tend to prefer Abadar or Lambatuin.

Very rarely, ikeshtis become zealous prophets who receive visions and wisdom from unspecified forces. The source of these premonitions is a mystery, although the naturally appearing sigils on their scales suggest Eloritu or the newly hatched Newborn may be involved, or else something foul from the fringes of existence or the dark places between the stars.

Popular Edicts

respect the mental health of yourself and others, protect your community, reuse and repurpose goods and materials, be thrifty

Popular Anathema

suppress or ignore your emotions, betray those close to you, cause wanton destruction, be wasteful

Ikeshti Mechanics

Hit Points 6

Size Small

Speed 25 Feet; Climb 15 Feet

Attribute Boosts Dexterity, Intelligence, Free

Attribute Flaw Wisdom

Languages Akitonian, Common, One regional language of your choice.

Additional languages equal to your Intelligence modifier (if it's positive). Choose from the list of common languages and any other languages to which you have access (such as the languages prevalent on your home world).

Traits

  • humanoid, ikeshti

Wasteland Survivor

Kalo.name: 卡洛 Kalo
Kalo.text:
卡洛鱼人来自卡洛-马霍伊——布雷瑟达的一颗水域丰饶的卫星。自从他们推翻邪恶的林虫压迫者统治以来，便一直在相对和平与安全的环境中生活。尽管最后的林虫早已在许久以前被消灭，卡洛鱼人依然对军事训练与传统保持警惕。他们令人生畏的名声，使卡洛鱼人士兵与雇佣兵始终供不应求。

如今，卡洛鱼人在海底温暖地热喷口周围聚集而成的稠密扩张城市中繁荣发展。艺术家、表演者、发明家，以及定制商品的创造者，都能在卡洛-马霍伊的海洋中找到现成市场。访客从协约世界各地而来，探索这些明亮、多彩都市中心的画廊、时装屋与海底景观。

如果你想要一名坚韧、富有创意且独一无二的角色，不妨尝试扮演一名卡洛鱼人！

你或许会……

  • 因探索自然之美而获得灵感。
  • 在意想不到的地方结交朋友。
  • 将个性化你的装备作为自我表达的方式。

其他人可能会……

  • 向你请教风格建议。
  • 认为你在陆地上不适应环境。
  • 畏惧你的武艺。

外貌描述

卡洛鱼人是水生类人生物，身覆鳞片，通常以蓝色和绿色为主，但有时也会呈现浅粉色或灰色。成年个体身高约五到六英尺，但由于体型纤细且骨骼由软骨构成，其平均体重略低于100磅。由半透明薄膜构成的翼膜连接着卡洛鱼人的手臂与双腿，帮助他们在水中轻松游动。感知触须覆盖其脸部下半部，环绕着长满细小针状牙齿的口部。他们的大眼能够独立移动与聚焦，以同时感知掠食者与猎物，并散发出微弱柔和的光辉，卡洛鱼人会借此无声地彼此传递信号。一排装饰性棘刺从额头向后扫去，延伸至颅骨底部。卡洛鱼人通过鳃呼吸，而鳃只能在水中发挥作用，因此他们在陆地上通常会佩戴充满液体的头盔、呼吸器，或全套环境服。即便如此，他们仍必须定期浸入水中，否则鳞片会因干燥而痛苦地剥落。

艺术性的自我表达对卡洛鱼人文化极其重要，而这一点在他们的身体上体现得最为明显。最常见的改造对象是卡洛鱼人的装饰性棘刺，他们经常对其进行穿孔、染色或塑形，而且往往会在一生中多次改变其样貌。

卡洛鱼人也经常改造自己的感知触须、翼膜以及生物荧光眼睛，而他们常会用染色镜片改变眼睛的颜色。此外，卡洛鱼人工业还开发出了极其高效的水下化妆品，许多卡洛鱼人都会频繁且夸张地加以使用。

社群

如今，大多数卡洛鱼人生活在人口稠密的大都市中，享受着让生活极其舒适且安全的魔法科技便利设施。这些城市以提供充足能量的温暖地热喷口为中心，是卡洛-马霍伊上繁忙的工业、艺术与科技枢纽，而且大多仍在不断扩张。色彩鲜明、光彩夺目的这些城市，对本地居民和游客而言都是充满吸引力的灯塔；人们蜂拥而至，寻求刺激、财富以及志同道合的社群。卡洛鱼人城市通常设有广阔的气泡区，内含可供呼吸的大气，使其对来自外星、以空气呼吸的访客更加友好。

对卡洛鱼人而言，生活即艺术。卡洛-马霍伊令人屏息的建筑、开创性的艺术，以及新潮时尚，吸引着来自协约世界各地的访客；其中尤以每年将大因扎变成独特设计天堂的深海盛宴最为著名。由于艺术追求受到高度重视，许多卡洛鱼人也极其看重日用品的定制化与独特版本。围绕定制服装、餐具、家具、载具、武器以及无数其他事物的手工产业在卡洛鱼人文化中蓬勃发展，而这些定制商品无论在卡洛-马霍伊内外都需求旺盛。

部分得益于丰富的地热能量与原初魔法来源，卡洛鱼人社群很快形成了保护家园自然奇观的政治意志。对用于废物回收与污染物封存的魔法科技系统的大规模公共投资，使卡洛-马霍伊的海洋免于遭受许多快速工业化世界的命运。卡洛鱼人将大片荒野划为自然保护区，尽管一些活动人士认为卡洛鱼人迅速的城市扩张构成了威胁，并推动——有时甚至以暴力方式——实施更严格的限制。虽然大多数人嘴上都承认保护环境的重要性，但许多企业实体与贸易组织私下里却游说要求扩大自然资源的开发权限并放宽限制，把那些更为激进的环保主义者视为其利润底线的严重威胁。

示例姓名

卡洛鱼人的父母常以知名人物——无论真实还是虚构——的名字为孩子命名，并以某种方式加以改动，使其独一无二。卡洛鱼人名字示例包括Huur-Lithu、Olaavhu、Shavhiul、Shuuthul和Ulluumoth。

信仰

在林虫于卡洛-马霍伊肆虐的时期，它们鼓励对上古者之一拉伽达恩的崇拜，并认为其是自己的始祖。海蚀洞中的古老雕刻表明，在拉伽达恩的子嗣到来之前数千年，卡洛鱼人就已经在崇拜拉伽达恩。这导致了一种理论：卡洛鱼人最初是从第一世界来到卡洛-马霍伊的。

如今，拉伽达恩的信仰已大为衰落，许多人将其视为族群长期受压迫的残余。不过，仍有一些人坚持这一传统信仰，将拉伽达恩视为需要安抚的力量而向其祈祷。其他信仰也在民众中零星流行，包括艾洛里图、维丹，以及宗-莎琳。

常见典范

颂扬并鼓励万事万物中的多样性；让你所到之处都比你初见时更加美丽

常见禁忌

背叛盟约；不尊重他人的自我表达

卡洛鱼人机制

生命值 6

体型 中型

速度 20尺；游泳30尺

属性加值 敏捷、魅力、自由

属性缺陷 体质

语言 等于你的智力调整值（如果为正值）。从常见语言列表以及你可接触到的任何其他语言中选择（例如你母星上通行的语言）。

额外语言 通用语、卡洛语

特质

  • aquatic
  • humanoid
  • kalo

感官 黑暗视觉

水分补给

卡洛鱼人传承

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.heritages.Item.v1FRU6XUr7QZsG6d]{Bloom-Born Kalo}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.heritages.Item.Ey2iWNKXd8rzHP2J]{Deep Kalo}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.heritages.Item.ZAOKze0PTYPPbUua]{Electric Kalo}

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.heritages.Item.8xJLb7SmPydkTzfG]{Outworlder Kalo}

Kalos are from Kalo-Mahoi, a watery moon of Bretheda where they've lived in relative peace and safety since throwing off the rule of wicked linnorm oppressors. Though the last of the linnorms were killed long ago, kalos remain vigilant in military training and traditions. Their fearsome reputation keeps kalo soldiers and mercenaries in high demand.

Today, kalos thrive in dense, expanding cities clustered around warm geothermal vents on the ocean floor. Artists, performers, inventors, and creators of custom goods find a ready market in Kalo-Mahoi's seas. Visitors come from all over the Pact Worlds to explore the galleries, fashion houses, and submarine vistas of these bright, colorful urban centers.

If you want a character who's tough, creative, and one of a kind, try playing a kalo!

You Might...

  • Be inspired by exploring the beauty of nature.
  • Make friends in unexpected places.
  • Personalize your equipment as a means of self-expression.

Others Probably...

  • Ask you for style advice.
  • Assume you're out of your element on land.
  • Fear your martial prowess.

Physical Description

Kalos are aquatic humanoids covered in scales that appear predominantly blue and green, though occasionally pale pink or gray as well. They range from five to six feet tall as adults, but their slight builds and cartilaginous skeletons mean they average just under 100 pounds in weight. Wings of translucent membrane connect kalos' arms and legs, helping them to move effortlessly through the water. Sensory tendrils cover the lower halves of their faces, surrounding mouths full of small, needlelike teeth. Their large eyes can move and focus independently to sense predators and prey alike and faintly glow with a soft luminescence that kalos use as a means of silently signaling each other. An array of decorative spines sweeps back from their forehead down through the base of their skull. Kalos breathe through gills that only function in water, so they typically wear fluid-filled helmets, respirators, or full environmental suits when on land. Even so, they must periodically immerse themselves in water lest their scales dry out and painfully flake away.

Artistic self-expression is very important to kalo cultures, and nowhere is it more prevalent than on kalo bodies. The most common target of alteration are a kalo's decorative spines, which they frequently pierce, color, or shape, often changing them many times over the course of a lifetime.

Kalos also frequently modify their sensory tendrils and wings as well as their bioluminescent eyes, which they often recolor with tinted lenses. In addition, kalo industry has developed extremely effective underwater makeup compounds, of which many kalos make frequent and dramatic use.

Society

Today, most kalos live in densely populated metropolises with access to magitech amenities that make life extremely comfortable and safe. Centered around warm geothermal vents that provide ample energy for their needs, cities on Kalo-Mahoi are busy hubs of industry, art, and technology, and most continue to expand. Colorful and bright, these cities are welcoming beacons to locals and tourists who flock to them seeking excitement, fortune, and like-minded communities. Kalo cities usually have extensive bubble districts filled with breathable atmosphere that makes them more hospitable to air-breathing visitors from offworld.

For kalos, life is art. Kalo-Mahoi's breathtaking architecture, groundbreaking art, and fresh fashion draw visitors from around the Pact Worlds, especially the Deep Gala that transforms Grand Inza into a uniquely designed paradise every year. Because of the value placed on artistic pursuits, many kalos highly value customized and unique versions of everyday items. Industries based on crafting bespoke clothing, cutlery, furniture, vehicles, weapons, and myriad other things thrive in kalo culture, and these custom goods are in high demand both on and off Kalo-Mahoi.

Thanks in part to abundant sources of geothermal energy and primal magic, kalo society quickly developed the political will to preserve the natural wonders of their home. Significant public investment into magitech systems for waste recycling and pollutant sequestration prevented the waters of Kalo- Mahoi from suffering the fate of many rapidly industrializing worlds. Kalos designate vast swaths of wilderness as nature preserves, though some activists see the kalos' rapid urban growth as a threat and push, sometimes violently, for more stringent restrictions. Though most pay lip service to the importance of conservation, many corporate entities and trade organizations quietly lobby for expanded access to natural resources and loosened restrictions, viewing the more ardent conservationists as dire threats to their bottom lines.

Sample Names

Kalo parents often name their children after well-known figures, real or fictional, and alter the name in some way to make it unique. Example kalo names include Huur-Lithu, Olaavhu, Shavhiul, Shuuthul, and Ulluumoth.

Beliefs

During their time wreaking havoc on Kalo-Mahoi, linnorms encouraged the worship of Ragadahn of the Eldest, who they consider to be their progenitor. Ancient carvings in sea caves demonstrate that kalos were worshipping Ragadahn for millennia before the arrival of his children. This has led to theories that kalos originally came to Kalo-Mahoi from the First World.

Today, Ragadahn's faith is greatly diminished, many seeing it as a remnant of their people's long oppression. Some still hold to the traditional faith, however, praying to Ragadahn as a force to be appeased. Other faiths enjoy scattered popularity among the population, including Eloritu, Weydan, and Zon-Shelyn.

Popular Edicts

celebrate and encourage diversity in all things, leave everywhere you go more beautiful than you found it

Popular Anathema

betray an alliance, disrespect another's self-expression

Kalo Mechanics

Hit Points 6

Size Medium

Speed 20 Feet; Swim 30 Feet

Attribute Boosts Dexterity, Charisma, Free

Attribute Flaw Constitution

Languages equal to your, Intelligence modifier (if it's positive)., Choose from the list of common, languages and any other languages, to which you have access (such as, the languages prevalent on your, home world).

Additional languages Common Kalo

Traits

  • aquatic
  • humanoid
  • kalo

Senses Darkvision

Hydration

Murder in Metal City.name: 金属之城谋杀案 Murder in Metal City

Khizar.name: 希扎尔 Khizar
Khizar.text:
基扎尔是智慧植物，与自然和谐共生，并学会了通过心灵感应与其他生物交流。

基扎尔进化于卡斯卓韦乌库兰的深邃丛林，最初是食腐植物，依靠粗浅的移情感知和缓慢而耐心的动作猎食垂死的动物。许多科学家认为，它们与克萨里克出自同一祖系演化支，这两者在基因上相似，但克萨里克缺乏复杂的神经系统，因此基扎尔才能发展出真正的智慧以及与其他物种交流的能力。

基扎尔在卡斯卓韦繁盛生息，但也已扩散到其他生态环境，例如阿巴隆的冰渊以及伊达里号内封闭的自然保护区。基扎尔常常被吸引到领导岗位，在那里培育社群成长；或者建立荒野旅舍和前哨站，帮助他人学会以安全且尊重的方式享受户外环境。

如果你想扮演一名坚韧、耐心并与自然有深刻连接的角色，你就应该选择基扎尔。

你可能会……

  • 关心环境，或花时间待在自然中。
  • 说话和行动缓慢，或在做决定时不紧不慢。
  • 在季节变化时，或搬到新的气候环境时，显著改变自己的态度与外表。

其他人多半会……

  • 注意到自然如何影响你的外表与健康。
  • 在荒野中向你寻求指引。
  • 对你的外貌、文化和起源感到好奇。

外貌描述

当一名基扎尔发芽时，它们起初是一个微小发光的种荚，周围环绕着精致的格状结构。数周之内，柔嫩的新芽会从种荚中长出，并攀附到它们找到的任何营养来源上——通常是一具动物尸体，或是照料幼年基扎尔的亲代。随着时间推移，幼苗最初的嫩芽会变得坚韧并伸长为藤蔓状的生长体。基扎尔幼苗在生命最初的几年里学习控制这些藤蔓，几乎不会独自移动。在此期间，幼苗不需要看护，只需要营养。亲代常常会把幼苗和一具动物尸体，以及某种娱乐来源——例如播放着 Zo! 经典作品重播的数据板——一起丢下很长一段时间，自己则去寻找家族的下一处食物来源。

一旦基扎尔稳定为自己偏爱的身体形态，它们便进入树苗阶段。树苗会将藤蔓长成强壮而柔韧的肢体。有些树苗开始将身体训练成熟悉的形状，以让其他人更安心，长出两条手臂、两条腿、一个躯干，以及一个位于头部位置的大型种荚；但许多基扎尔更偏爱模仿自己最初扎根之地荒野中的动植物形态。

在15到30岁之间的某个时候，树苗会成熟并开花。第一次开花对许多基扎尔群体而言具有重要文化意义，象征着步入成年。基扎尔在开花后很少再改变形态，而这一生命阶段也是它们最有可能踏上冒险之路，或在偏远的荒野社群中定居的时候，例如阿巴隆冰渊那与世隔绝、冰封的丛林。

基扎尔天生能活数百年，但也可能死于疾病或伤害。野火和非法采伐活动每年都会导致基扎尔死亡。

社群

一些基扎尔过着独居生活，彼此竞争资源，只有在共同保护刚发芽的幼苗时才会聚在一起；它们远非终生配对，这种松散结合通常最多只持续几年，随后家庭群体便会散开，重新回到孤独的生活中搜寻资源，并通过复杂的远距离信号交流，例如培植苔藓和敲击树干。

但在乌库兰腹地和其他隐秘之处，大片基扎尔林苑栖居于由其祖先保存下来的树皮构成的活体城市中，与克萨里克共同生活；后者作为宠物和狩猎伙伴与它们共存。

对它们而言，离开原始荒野去追寻新天地，或在远离家园之处发芽的基扎尔，就是“野种”。野种遍布银河各地。

所有基扎尔生来都会说同一种缓慢、吱嘎作响的语言，其他物种几乎不可能复现。无论在哪里长大，基扎尔似乎都共享一套成语典籍，其中大多以季节变化来象征死亡、情感等抽象概念。通常，基扎尔的名字就来自这套词汇。多数基扎尔会在生命最初10年内从照料者那里获得名字，但也有些会在第一次开花时为自己取名。

示例名称

月晕警兆、转叶、北坡苔藓、旱灾前的最后一场雨、绿色蝗灾预言

信念

基扎尔过着漫长而缓慢的生活，专注于活在当下。有些会冥想现实的本质，或与神圣或原初力量交流，但基扎尔认为宗教教条既无谓地束缚人，又令人厌烦。相反，大多数基扎尔更倾向于通过丰富而深广的人生经历来寻求启迪。

常见典范

享受当下，恣意生长，保护自然

常见禁忌

伤害活物，剥削自然资源，阻碍成长

基扎尔机制

生命值 8

体型 中型

速度 20尺；攀爬 20尺

属性增强 体质、感知、自由

属性缺陷 敏捷

语言 通用语、基扎尔语、你自选的一种地区语言

额外语言 数量等于你的智力调整值（如果为正值）。从常见语言列表以及你能接触到的任何其他语言中选择（例如你所在地区流行的语言）。

特质

  • 基扎尔
  • 植物

有限心灵感应

Khizars are intelligent plants who live in harmony with nature and have learned to communicate telepathically with other creatures.

Khizars evolved in the deep jungles of Ukulam on Castrovel, beginning as carrion-feeding plants who hunted dying animals with crude empathic senses and slow, patient movements. Many scientists believe they evolved from the same parent clade as ksariks, who share genetic similarities but lack the complex nervous systems that have allowed khizars to develop true sapience and the ability to communicate with other species.

Khizars thrive on Castrovel, but they've also spread to other ecosystems such as Aballon's Ice Wells and the Idari's contained nature preserves. Khizars often gravitate to positions of leadership where they can nurture community growth or establish wilderness lodges and outposts where they can help others learn to safely and respectfully enjoy the outdoors.

If you want to play a resilient, patient character with a deep connection to nature, you should play a khizar.

You Might...

  • Care about the environment or spend time in nature.
  • Speak and move slowly or take your time making decisions.
  • Drastically alter your attitude and appearance when the season changes or if you move to a new climate.

Others Probably...

  • Notice how nature influences your appearance and health.
  • Turn to you for guidance in the wilderness.
  • Are curious about your appearance, culture, and origins.

Physical Description

When a khizar germinates, they begin as a tiny glowing seedpod surrounded by a delicate lattice. Over a period of weeks, tender shoots sprout from the seedpod and cling onto any source of nutrients they find usually an animal carcass or a parent caring for the baby khizar. Over time, the seedling's first shoots toughen and lengthen into vine-like growths. Khizar seedlings spend their first years of life learning to control the vines and don't move much on their own. During this time, seedlings don't need supervision, only nutrition. It's common for a parent to abandon seedlings with an animal carcass and a source of entertainment such as a datapad playing reruns of Zo!'s classic productions for long periods while they search for their family's next source of food.

Once khizars settle into a favored body shape, they enter the sapling phase of life. The sapling grows their vines into strong, malleable limbs. Some saplings begin training their bodies into familiar shapes to put others at ease, growing two arms, two legs, a torso, and a large seedpod where a head would be, but many khizars prefer shapes that mimic the flora and fauna of the wilds where they first took root.

Sometime between 15 and 30 years of life, a sapling matures and flowers. The first flowering is culturally significant for many khizar populations and signals the passage into adulthood. Khizars rarely change their forms after flowering, and this phase of life is when they're most likely to take up adventuring or settle down in a remote wilderness community, such as the isolated, frozen jungles of Aballon's Ice Wells.

Khizars naturally live for hundreds of years, but they can die of disease or injury. Wildfires and illegal logging operations are responsible for the deaths of khizars every year.

Society

Some khizars lead solitary lives, competing for resources and only coming together for the mutual protection of germinant seedlings far from mating for life, these loose pairings last a few years at most before the family group disperses to resume their lonely lives scrounging for resources and communicating through complex long-distance signals such as the cultivation of moss and trunk drumming.

But in the heart of Ukulam and other secluded places, vast groves of khizars dwell in living cities made from the preserved bark of their ancestors, alongside ksariks, who coexist as pets and hunting partners.

To them, a khizar who leaves the pristine wilds for new horizons or germinates far from home is a wildseed. Wildseeds are everywhere in the galaxy.

All khizars are born speaking the same slow, creaking language that's nearly impossible for other species to replicate. Khizars seem to share a codex of idioms regardless of where they grew up, mostly based around symbolizing abstract concepts like death and emotions with seasonal changes. Typically, khizar names come from this lexicon. Most khizars earn a name from a caretaker in the first 10 years of their life, but some choose their own names at their first flowering.

Sample Names

Moon Halo Warning, Turning Leaves, North Face Moss, Last Rain Before the Drought, Green Locust Prophec

Beliefs

Khizars live long, slow lives spent existing in the moment. Some meditate on the nature of reality or commune with divine or primal forces, but khizars find religious dogma needlessly confining and tedious. Instead, most khizars prefer to seek enlightenment through a depth and breadth of life experience.

Popular Edicts

enjoy the moment, grow wild, protect nature

Popular Anathema

harm a living creature, exploit natural resources, hinder growth

Khizar Mechanics

Hit Points 8

Size Medium

Speed 20 Feet; Climb 20 Feet

Attribute Boosts Constitution, Wisdom, Free

Attribute Flaw Dexterity

Languages Common, Khizar, Regional language of your choice

Additional languages equal to your Intelligence modifier (if it's positive). Choose from the list of common languages and any other languages to which you have access (such as the languages prevalent in your region).

Traits

  • khizar
  • plant

Limited Telepathy

Sarcesian.name: 萨瑟蝶人 Sarcesian
Sarcesian.text:
萨瑟蝶人是流散带的继承者——达米尔与伊欧沃两颗已毁灭星球居民的后裔，而如今这两颗星球的残骸构成了流散带小行星带。他们既为自身的历史感到自豪，又热切拥抱更光明的未来；萨瑟蝶人是富有耐心的幸存者，崇尚个人主义与探索的乐趣，同时也维系着与自身社群和家园的连接。出于对旅行的天性热爱，萨瑟蝶人常常训练成为飞行员与领航员；很少有萨瑟蝶人会对驾驶舱感到不自在。尽管他们大多积极乐观、性情随和，但有些人对邻近星球伊俄克斯的居民怀有与生俱来的怀疑，因为在久远的过去，正是一场来自伊俄克斯人的攻击摧毁了他们祖先的母星世界。

萨瑟蝶人能够通过暂停呼吸并保护自己的身体免受虚空危险来在太空中生存。他们会显现出一对由纯粹能量构成的、如蝴蝶般的翅膀，这对翅膀能作为太阳帆运作。萨瑟蝶人飞行时的景象壮丽非凡，最早与他们相遇的矮人与伊斯基矿工曾将这类相遇描述为见到了“虚空天使”。

在一生之中，萨瑟蝶人会学会控制环绕自身的各种能量——尤其是源自太空的能量。许多萨瑟蝶人会显现魔法力量、操纵太阳流，或释放金色的光。

如果你想扮演一名性情自由的太空旅者，你就该选择萨瑟蝶人。

你或许会……

  • 无论多久才回去一次，都会对家园怀有深厚的连接。
  • 很容易交到朋友，但会避开权力职位。
  • 喜欢仰望星空，并欣赏太空的宁静。

其他人多半会……

  • 以为你可以无限期地在太空中生存，并疑惑你的翅膀为何不总是可见。
  • 觉得你不够负责，或者飘忽不定。
  • 欣赏你积极的看法与随和的态度。

外貌描述

萨瑟蝶人在漫长岁月中适应了低重力与稀薄空气的生活环境，变得敏捷却脆弱。他们极其高挑纤细，身高通常在10到15英尺之间，四肢细长，体态轻盈纤弱。大多数萨瑟蝶人身体柔韧，能够挤进令人惊讶的小空间中。

他们没有毛发，皮肤灰色，耳朵尖长，眼睛巨大而发光。他们视觉与听觉敏锐，但也习惯了长时间安静地旅行。他们是极富表现力的交流者，说话时会配合夸张的面部表情以及手部与身体动作。萨瑟蝶人常会学习自己所说语言的手语版本，以进一步便于无声交流。

萨瑟蝶人会显现辉煌的能量之翼，并通过暂停呼吸来承受短暂的太空出行。

尽管萨瑟蝶人在服饰上没有基于时尚的禁忌，但他们往往穿着贴身、覆盖身体大部分区域的衣物——这对那些经常在低重力与零重力环境之间切换的人来说是务实的选择。由于他们的皮肤很少外露，萨瑟蝶人很少纹身或进行其他装饰性的皮肤改造，除了脸部和头顶部位。大多数人都偏爱戏剧化的妆容。定居于行星表面的萨瑟蝶人，以及远离流散带生活的萨瑟蝶人，穿着通常更大胆，也更少考虑实用性；只要环境限制较少，他们就会顺从个人的一时兴致。

社群

萨瑟蝶人是爱好和平、崇尚自由意志的民族，他们不喜欢正式法律。他们在自由与无政府状态之间维持着脆弱的平衡，会反抗那些侵犯其个人自由与选择的权威以及社群期待，但对于更微妙、更遥远的界限却几乎不会抱怨地接受。他们不愿将自己的信念强加于他人——哪怕对象是同为萨瑟蝶人的族人。事实上，大多数萨瑟蝶人宁愿彻底避开领导角色与其他掌权职位。比起叛逆或为改革奔走游说，他们更可能选择逃离问题。虽然他们不理解秩序与一致性的必要性（多数萨瑟蝶人觉得那令人窒息），但他们也不会怨怪那些享受其中的人，更不会努力去推翻它。

萨瑟蝶人天性自由；他们想做什么就做什么，想何时做就何时做，并相信每个人都应当拥有同样的权利。但这种个人主义并不总是意味着冲动或难以预测。萨瑟蝶人值得信赖，而且许多人会从日常规律中获得安慰，只要那是个人选择，而非被强加于身。

萨瑟蝶人重视家庭与社群，珍视朋友如同珍视自己的骨肉至亲。萨瑟蝶人并没有典型或被期待的伴侣模式——相反，每个萨瑟蝶人都追随自己的内心，将每段关系都视作独特的经历。家庭单元与婚姻都很少见。大多数孩子会在育幼星球上由受过训练的育幼者抚养长大——这些照料者与教育者因其耐心、勤勉与才能而深受尊敬。尽管这种育儿方式较为放手，大多数萨瑟蝶人仍会与家人建立积极且持续一生的关系。

示例名字

Alzarin, Auvirre, Centuria, Dezarin, Elgeni, Favienn, Fayetta, Melisar, Mengian, Mirai, Nacoile, Nefain, Novarri, Nykiti, Risiria, Sesiss, Visavra, Vox, Yex, Zunar, Zwarna, Zyym

信念

萨瑟蝶人是性情自由、崇尚自由意志的人，他们重视个人独立，却不觉得有必要将自己的看法强加于他人。即使他们很少与家人和社群频繁往来，他们仍重视这些关系，而且交友很快。萨瑟蝶人往往看重诚实与奉献，但不会为此牺牲自己——萨瑟蝶人很少会让荣誉之类的概念或他人的看法驱使自己做出糟糕决定，或去做任何自己不想做的事。

作为个体，萨瑟蝶人往往会接纳任何引起自己兴趣的神祇或哲学。作为一个族群，萨瑟蝶人更容易被拥抱旅行与独立的神祇所吸引——尤其是维丹。由于他们与外层太空及彼方虚空有着天生的连接，再加上他们在魔法创新方面的历史，像伊布拉、艾洛瑞图，甚至吞噬者这样的神祇在他们之中都颇受欢迎。

常见典范

拥抱你的兴趣，遨游群星，保护你关心的人们与社群

常见禁忌

将你的看法强加于他人，向权威低头，迎合你并不认同的期待与理念

萨瑟蝶人机制

生命值 8

体型 大型

速度 25尺

属性增强 敏捷、魅力、自由

属性缺陷 力量

语言 通用语、萨瑟蝶人语、萨瑟蝶人语（手语）

额外语言 等于你的智力调整值（若为正值）。从常见语言列表中选择，并可额外选择你能够接触到的任何其他语言（例如你所在地区通行的语言）。

特征

  • 人形生物
  • 萨瑟蝶人

能量之翼

Sarcesians are the inheritors of the Diaspora— descendants of the inhabitants of two destroyed planets called Damiar and Iovo, which now form the Diaspora asteroid belt. At once proud of their history and keen to embrace a brighter future, sarcesians are patient survivors who embrace individualism and the joys of exploration while maintaining connections to their communities and homes. Motivated by a natural love of travel, sarcesians often train as pilots and navigators; rare is the sarcesian who isn't comfortable in the cockpit. Despite their positive outlooks and easygoing natures, some harbor an innate suspicion of inhabitants of the neighboring planet Eox, for it was an Eoxian attack that destroyed their ancestral home worlds in ages long past.

Sarcesians can survive in space by suspending their respiration and shielding their bodies from the dangers of the void. They manifest a pair of butterfly-like wings made of pure energy that function as solar sails. The sight of a sarcesian in flight is magnificent, and the first dwarven and ysoki miners who met them described encounters with “void angels”.

Throughout their lives, sarcesians learn control over the energies surrounding them—particularly energy of cosmic origin. Many sarcesians manifest magical powers, manipulate solar currents, or produce golden light.

If you want to play a character who is a free-spirited spacefarer, you should play a sarcesian.

You Might...

  • Feel a deep connection to home, regardless of how often you pay it a visit.
  • Make friends easily but avoid positions of power.
  • Enjoy stargazing and appreciate the serenity of space.

Others Probably...

  • Assume you can survive in space indefinitely and question why your wings aren't always visible.
  • Consider you irresponsible or flighty.
  • Appreciate your positive outlook and easygoing attitude.

Physical Description

Sarcesians have adapted over the ages to live in low gravity and thin air, becoming nimble but frail. Extremely tall and slender, sarcesians stand between 10 and 15 feet tall and have spindly, elongated limbs and delicate frames. Most are flexible and capable of fitting into surprisingly small spaces.

They are hairless, with gray skin, pointed ears, and large, luminous eyes. They have keen vision and hearing, but are accustomed to traveling in silence for long periods. They are expressive communicators, punctuating speech with exaggerated facial expressions and hand and body movements. It's common for sarcesians to learn the signed versions of any languages they speak to further facilitate silent communication.

Sarcesians manifest glorious energy wings and suspend their respiration to endure jaunts in space.

While sarcesians have no fashion-based taboos, they tend to wear form-fitting attire that covers most of their bodies—a practical choice for those who frequently switch between low gravity and zero gravity environments. As their skin is rarely on display, sarcesians rarely get tattoos or other cosmetic dermal modifications save for those on the face and pate. Most are fond of dramatic makeup. World-bound sarcesians and those who live far from the Diaspora tend to be more audacious and less practical in their attire, following their individual whims whenever their environment is less restrictive.

Society

Sarcesians are a peaceful, libertarian people who dislike formal laws. They maintain a precarious balance between freedom and anarchy, pushing back against authority and societal expectations that infringe upon their personal freedoms and choices, but accepting more subtle and distant boundaries with little complaint. They're averse to imposing their own beliefs on others—even their fellow sarcesians. In fact, most prefer to avoid taking on leadership roles and other positions of power altogether. They're more likely to flee a problem than they are to rebel or lobby for reform. While they don't understand the need for order and conformity (most sarcesians find it suffocating), they don't begrudge those who enjoy it, nor do they strive to overturn it.

Sarcesians are free-spirited; they do what they want when they want to and believe that everyone deserves to do the same. But this individualism doesn't always equate to spontaneity or unpredictability. Sarcesians are reliable, and many take comfort in routine so long as it's a personal choice and not imposed upon them.

Sarcesians value family and community, cherishing their friends as much as their own flesh and blood. There is no typical or expected sarcesian coupling—rather, each sarcesian follows their own heart, treating every relationship as its own unique experience. Family units and marriages are rare. Most children are raised on a crèche world by trained crèche-minders— caretakers and educators deeply respected for their patience, diligence, and talents. Despite this hands-off parenting, most develop positive, lifelong relationships with their families.

Sample Names

Alzarin, Auvirre, Centuria, Dezarin, Elgeni, Favienn, Fayetta, Melisar, Mengian, Mirai, Nacoile, Nefain, Novarri, Nykiti, Risiria, Sesiss, Visavra, Vox, Yex, Zunar, Zwarna, Zyym

Beliefs

Sarcesians are free-spirited libertarians who value their personal independence but don't feel the need to enforce their opinions on others. They value their families and communities even if they rarely engage with them, and they make friends quickly. Sarcesians tend to value honesty and dedication, but not to their detriment—a sarcesian rarely lets concepts like honor or the opinions of others drive them into making a poor decision or doing anything they don't want to do.

Sarcesians as individuals tend to embrace whatever gods or philosophies catch their interests. As a people, sarcesians are drawn to gods who embrace travel and independence— particularly Weydan. Thanks to their innate connection to outer space and the void beyond as well as to a history of magical innovation, gods like Ibra, Eloritu, and even the Devourer are popular among them.

Popular Edicts

embrace your interests, travel the stars, protect the people and communities you care about

Popular Anathema

impose your opinions on others, bow to authority, conform to expectations and ideals you disagree with

Sarcesian Mechanics

Hit Points 8

Size Large

Speed 25 Feet

Attribute Boosts Dexterity, Charisma, Free

Attribute Flaw Strength

Languages Common, Sarcesian, Sarcesian (signed)

Additional languages equal to your Intelligence modifier (if positive). Choose from the list of common languages and any other languages to which you have access (such as the languages prevalent in your region).

Traits

  • humanoid
  • sarcesian

Energy Wings

Shobhad.name: 肖布哈德 Shobhad
Shobhad.text:
绍巴德人起源于阿基顿星球，自远古以来便生活在这颗寒冷而干旱的星球上，凭借决心、坚忍与自豪，征服了怪物横行的沙漠与严酷无情的冰川地貌。

绍巴德人既凶猛又内敛，在战斗中坚定不移，在休憩时沉默寡言。这些常见的性格特质，再加上他们令人望而生畏的体格，使他们落下了不友善且危险的名声，不过更准确地说，他们只是谨慎而沉稳。绍巴德人通常对外来者兴趣不大，但他们与阿基顿伊修奇人共享的历史在这两个物种之间建立了牢固的联系，而绍巴德太空旅行者往往偏好由伊修奇工匠打造的近战武器、护甲与太空服。

绍巴德人以精于使用长步枪而闻名；这种弹道武器精准而强力，而拥有四条手臂的绍巴德人尤其适合驾驭它。尽管这种标志性武器是任何持用者的骄傲，但它远非他们使用的唯一武器；强大的绍巴德战士也热衷于挥舞各种双手武器，从牙刃和战锤到火焰喷射器与机枪，不一而足。即便在猝不及防、手无寸铁的情况下，绍巴德人也因其残暴却高效的徒手武术而在协约世界广受敬重。

如果你想扮演一名体格魁梧、适应荒野、同时拥有安静、冷静且令人胆寒气质的角色，你就应该选择绍巴德人。

你可能会……

  • 比起拥挤的城市或其他人口密集地区，更喜欢未经驯化的开阔地带。
  • 擅长那些能让双手忙起来的爱好，比如雕刻、编织或塑形。
  • 为自己对装备和私人物品的保养与维护感到自豪，并蔑视那些不尊重自己物品的人。

其他人多半会……

  • 因听闻过关于你族人尚武天性的夸张传言，而带着不安的敬意对待你。
  • 将你视为向导或护卫，欣赏你的强大力量与敏锐洞察。
  • 把你沉静的性格与冷峻不屈的神情误认为轻蔑或傲慢。

外貌描述

绍巴德人拥有令人印象深刻的体型，身高在10到12英尺之间，肌肉发达。他们的肤色范围包括灰尘般的灰色、鲜亮的绿色以及褪色的绿松石色。他们有四条强壮的手臂，可借此使用各种对其他物种而言通常过大或过于笨重的武器，不论对方是否也有多条手臂皆然。绍巴德人没有毛发，下颚长有显著的、如獠牙般突出的犬齿。

阿基顿上的绍巴德-内赫通常穿着轻便，更偏好装备背带与实用服装，以在长途跋涉时兼顾行动便利和对阿基顿恶劣环境的防护。他们天生就已适应阿基顿刺骨的寒冷，因此在穿着上很少特别考虑御寒。通常只有宗教领袖才会穿戴格外华丽的衣着。

不过，对实用性的偏爱并不等于朴素无华。绍巴德人欣赏艺术性，但厌恶浮夸，并将物质上的过度视为愚蠢、自私与失荣的表现。因此，绍巴德人

喜爱那些兼具实用与艺术性的武器、衣物、装备及其他有用物品——它们都经过精心制作，并以能提升用途（或至少不妨碍使用）的方式细致装饰。绍巴德工匠以注重细节、风格历久弥新以及含蓄的艺术点缀而闻名。绍巴德-内赫偏爱手工制作的物件与装备，胜过批量生产的同类品，常常将珍爱的物品代代相传。绍巴德人还发展出一种独特的编结工艺，而他们的四只手尤其适合从事这种技艺。这种编结作品通常包含独特的结法与织法，用以表明某位绍巴德人的氏族、出身地以及其他血统细节。

社群

在他们的家园阿基顿上，绍巴德人以游牧氏族形式生活，规模从少数坚毅战士到数十人的庞大家庭不等，游走于阿基顿的荒野地区。大多数氏族沿着祖先领地中的传统路线迁徙，随季节变换而移动，以躲避恶劣天气、追踪猎物或采集食物。外来文明的缓慢侵蚀，以及掠夺者、盗匪与合作势力突然闯入他们的领地，都是对绍巴德-内赫生活方式的威胁；他们对此极为严肃看待，而这样的威胁往往会遭到武装反抗。事实上，这些外来侵入几乎是唯一能确保将彼此敌对的绍巴德氏族团结起来的方式。面对外部威胁时，即便延续数千年的宿怨与血仇也会被暂时搁置——先是绍巴德-内赫，再是绍巴德个体。

传统上，绍巴德氏族的领导权分为世俗职责与宗教职责。氏族领袖被称为ne-jak，负责处理领地或财产争端、选择斥候与营地、配给资源以及其他此类事务。万物有灵信仰的宗教领袖被称为ne-tal，他们与灵体交流并进行占卜，以为氏族联合提供指引，主持婚礼，担任接生圣者，并主持葬礼仪式。

通常来说，ne-jak与ne-tal的职责并不重叠。不过，如果某个氏族的ne-tal认为世俗领袖正带领氏族走向危险或不当的道路，或是年老体衰使得ne-jak无法充分履行职责，那么ne-tal可以号召氏族长老进行投票，罢黜该ne-jak。这样的行动极少有必要，因为绍巴德人自幼就被教导要将氏族置于个人之上。正因如此，若非有重大事故或事件迫使他们离开，很少有绍巴德人会选择永久脱离自己的氏族。放逐、灾难、跨氏族婚姻、饥荒与战争都属于最常见的诱因，氏族被外来势力摧毁也是如此。那些感到自己受召去旅行、冒险或见识更广阔银河的绍巴德人，往往会担任斥候或商旅；而那些寻求更永久分离的人，则倾向于与几名同族人一同脱离原氏族，分裂成一个新的氏族。氏族与这类分支氏族之间的关系，会因分裂的原因及其实现方式而异。

绍巴德人将强烈的独立性与对氏族、族人和母星的深切敬重结合在一起。他们相信，一个人有责任为自己的社群付出超过其所得；这种哲学在他们流动的游牧氏族中运作良好，但往往会让个别绍巴德人在高人口密度地区感到不安。在城市中，食物、饮水、交通和其他生活必需品都很容易获得，且往往免费发放；在这样的地方，绍巴德人很难理解自己究竟该如何做到付出大于所得。这个问题让许多其他物种误以为绍巴德人反社会，甚至反对文明概念，但事实几乎恰恰相反；有些绍巴德人只是难以理解，在一个无法以传统方式为他们创造贡献机会的社会结构里，自己该如何恰当地融入其中。

绍巴德人通常只有一个个人名字，长度为两到三个音节，且常带连字符；他们依靠编结工艺来标示自己的氏族归属、血统与其他联系。他们偏好那些在长距离呼喊时依然容易分辨的硬音。

示例名字

Aruk, Bo-Shek, Do-Jat, Jah-Tor, Katso, Naz-Er, Samdak, Tan-Takneh, Virek-Nol, Zaren-Ka

信仰

绍巴德人的宗教信仰主要属于万物有灵论；他们信奉体现各种概念、生物与地点的灵体与幽影。传统的阿基顿绍巴德人相信，就连行星也有灵魂，而人生的成功来自于理解并体现那种本质。他们说，阿基顿星球拥有安静而无情的灵魂，这要求它的人民也安静而无情；不过大多数绍巴德人都清楚，不该将这一哲学推向极端，以致使自己与族人或必要的盟友疏离。虽然绍巴德-内赫中有擅长与不同地域和行星上形形色色灵体沟通的秘术师，但大多数绍巴德人在进入一片新区域或拜访宗教场所时，至少都会表现出一点敬意，因为他们相信，不尊重任何灵体——无论它多么微小、无足轻重、古怪或异域——都可能招致长久的不幸。

他们对灵体的尊重，也是他们偏好长步枪的一个因素。虽然绍巴德人并不畏惧与敌人正面战斗，但他们的文化中对于用远程武器杀死一个看得见你的敌人，怀有某种顾虑。这与古老的荣誉观以及他们对灵性的理解有关；许多绍巴德人相信，或者至少担心，被不公正地杀死的对手之灵可能会追随他们并带来厄运。因此，绍巴德长步枪是一种射程惊人、精度出众的武器，足以在猎物或敌人还未能看清狙击手面孔之前就终结其生命。尽管有些人可能认为这纯属迷信，但绍巴德-内赫所敬奉的灵体确实能在其宗教领袖的魔法中得到体现，而他们狙击手的技艺与长寿传统在整个协约世界都备受推崇，这也暗示着这一观念中或许确有几分经验上的真实。

除了传统信仰之外，许多现代绍巴德人也选择崇拜诸神，其中韦丹尤其受欢迎。那些居住在大型都市中心、并非在氏族中长大，或已经抛下传统生活方式的绍巴德人，在宗教信仰上表现出更广泛的多样性，偏好贝斯玛拉、戴莫力图斯、吞噬者、Elotiru、Ibra、Lao Shu Po，甚至宗-莎琳。

常见戒律

掌握生存所需的技能，以免成为他人的负担；敬重赋予你生命的世界

常见禁忌

通过剥削性滥用资源来亵渎阿基顿或你称之为家园的其他星球；从社群中索取多于付出

绍巴德机制

生命值 8

体型 大型

速度 25英尺

属性增强 力量、感知、自由

属性缺陷 魅力

语言 阿基顿语、通用语、绍巴德语

额外语言 你选择的一种地区语言。额外语言数量等于你的智力调整值（若其为正值）。从常见语言列表以及你能够接触到的任何其他语言中选择（例如你母星上通行的语言）。

特征

  • humanoid, shobhad

适应寒冷的
