Shobhads originate on the planet Akiton and have lived on that cold, arid planet since time immemorial, mastering its monster-infested deserts and unforgiving glacial formations with determination, stoicism, and pride.

Simultaneously fierce and reserved, shobhads are implacable in battle and quiet at rest. These common personality traits, combined with their imposing physiques, have given them a reputation for being unwelcoming and dangerous, though it's more accurate to describe them as cautious and reserved. Shobhads generally have little use for outsiders, but their shared history with the ysoki of Akiton has created strong bonds between the two species, and shobhad spacefarers often prefer melee weapons, armor, and spacesuits created by ysoki artisans.

Shobhads are renowned for their expert marksmanship with their longrifles, highly accurate and powerful ballistic weapons that shobhads, with their four arms, are uniquely suited to wielding. While these signature weapons are the pride of any shobhad who wields one, they're far from the only one they utilize, and powerful shobhad warriors eagerly wield a variety of two-handed weapons ranging from fangblades and hammers to flamethrowers and machine guns. Even caught off- guard without a weapon, shobhads are respected across the Pact Worlds for their brutal but effective unarmed martial arts.

If you want a character that is physically imposing, at home in the wilderness, and possessed of a demeanor that is quiet, collected, and intimidating, you should play a shobhad.

You Might...

  • Prefer untamed and open spaces over crowded cities or other heavily populated areas.
  • Excel at hobbies that keep your hands occupied, such as carving, knitting, or sculpting.
  • Take pride in the care and upkeep of your equipment and personal possessions, scorning those who don't respect their own possessions.

Others Probably...

  • Treat you with uneasy respect, having heard wild rumors of your people's warlike nature.
  • Value you as a guide or protector, appreciating your great strength and keen insight.
  • Mistake your quiet nature and implacable mien for scornfulness or arrogance.

Physical Description

Shobhads have an impressive physical stature, standing between 10 and 12 feet tall with a muscular build. Their skin includes a range of colors from dusty gray, vibrant green, and faded turquoise. They have four powerful arms, which they which they use to wield a variety of weapons that would typically be too large or cumbersome for other species, multi-armed or otherwise. Shobhads are hairless and have pronounced, tusk-like canines in their lower jaw.

Shobhad-neh on Akiton typically go lightly clothed, preferring equipment harnesses and practical clothing that allows them to strike a balance between ease of movement during their long journeys and protection from Akiton's harsh conditions. They're naturally inured to Akiton's biting cold and thus give little consideration in their dress when it comes to the cold. Typically, only religious leaders wear notably ornate clothing.

Fondness for practicality doesn't equate with plainness, though. Shobhads appreciate artistry but frown upon frivolity and consider material excess a sign of foolishness, selfishness, and dishonor. Thus, shobhads

enjoy practical art weapons, clothing, equipment, and other useful items crafted with care and lovingly decorated in ways that enhance (or at least don't impede) use. Shobhad crafters are known for their attention to detail, timeless designs, and understated artistic flourishes. Shobhad-neh favor hand-made objects and equipment over mass-produced equivalents, often handing beloved objects down through the generations. Shobhads practice a unique type of braidwork that their four hands are uniquely suited to. This braidwork often includes unique knots and weaves indicating a given shobhad's clan, home of origin, and other lineal details.

Society

On their home world of Akiton, shobhads live in nomadic clans, varying in size from a handful of hardy warriors to dozens of sprawling families, which range across Akiton's wild regions. Most follow traditional routes through their ancestral territory, moving with the seasons to avoid inclement weather, follow game, or harvest food. The slow encroachment of foreign civilization and the sudden incursion of raiders, thieves, and corporations upon their territory is a threat to the shobhad-neh way of life, which they take very seriously a threat often met with armed resistance. In fact, these foreign intrusions are one of the only surefire ways to unite rival shobhad clans together. In the face of outside threats, even millennia-long grudges and blood feuds are put aside shobhad-neh before shobhad.

Traditionally, leadership of shobhad clans is divided into secular and religious responsibilities. Clan leaders, known as ne-jaks, handle issues like territory or property disputes, the choosing of scouts and campsites, rationing of resources, and other such affairs. Animistic religious leaders, called ne-tals, consult with spirits and perform auguries to provide guidance on clan unions, perform marriages, act as midwives, and conduct funeral rites.

Typically, the responsibilities of ne-jaks and ne-tals do not overlap, though a clan's ne-tal can call for a vote among the elders of a clan to depose a ne-jak if they believe that the secular leader is guiding the clan down a dangerous or inappropriate path, or if age and infirmity are preventing the ne-jak from performing their duties adequately. Such action is rarely necessary since shobhad are taught from a young age to value the clan over the individual. This outlook means that few shobhads choose to permanently leave their clan without some major incident or event to drive them away. Banishment, disaster, inter-clan marriage, starvation, and war number among the most common instigating events, as does the destruction of a clan by outside forces. Shobhads who feel called to travel, adventure, or see the wider galaxy tend to work as scouts or traders, while those looking for a more permanent division tend to break away from their clan alongside a few other clanmates, splitting into a new clan. The relationship between clans and such splinter clans varies depending upon the reason for the split and the method by which it was achieved.

Shobhads combine fierce independence with a deep reverence for their clan, people, and home world. They believe it's an individual's responsibility to give more to their community than they receive from it; this philosophy works well among their wandering nomadic clans but often makes individual shobhads uneasy in highly populated areas. In cities where food, water, transportation, and other necessities are readily available and often freely dispersed, it can be hard for a shobhad to understand how they can possibly contribute more than they are being given. This issue has led to many other species incorrectly believing that shobhads are antisocial or even opposed to concepts of civilization, though the truth is almost the opposite; some shobhads merely struggle with understanding how they can appropriately fit into a social structure that doesn't create opportunities for them to contribute in traditional ways.

Shobhads tend to have a single personal name, two to three syllables in length and often hyphenated, relying upon braidwork to denote their clan affiliations, lineage, and other ties. They favor harsh sounds that remain easily distinguishable when shouted over long distances.

Sample Names

Aruk, Bo-Shek, Do-Jat, Jah-Tor, Katso, Naz-Er, Samdak, Tan-Takneh, Virek-Nol, Zaren-Ka

Beliefs

Shobhad religious beliefs are primarily animistic, with a faith in spirits and apparitions that embody a wide variety of concepts, creatures, and locations. Traditional Akitonian shobhads believe that even planets have a soul and that success in life comes from understanding and embodying that essence. The planet Akiton, they say, has a quiet and unforgiving soul that demands a quiet and unforgiving people, though most shobhads know better than to take this philosophy to an extreme that would alienate them from their people or from necessary allies. While shobhad-neh have mystics who excel at communicating with the many and varied spirits of different locales and planets, most shobhads pay at least a small showing of respect when entering a new region or visiting a religious site, believing that disrespecting any spirit (no matter how small, insignificant, strange, or foreign) can lead to lasting misfortune.

Their respect for spirits is also an element of their preference for the longrifle. While shobhads do not shy from meeting an enemy in combat, there is a cultural misgiving related to killing an enemy who can see you with a ranged weapon. Tied to ancient views on honor and their spiritual nature, many shobhad believe, or at least worry, that the spirit of an opponent killed unfairly might follow them and bring bad luck. Thus, the shobhad longrifle is a weapon with exceptional range and accuracy capable of ending a life before their prey or foe can ever get a clear look at the sniper's face. Though some might view this as pure superstition, the existence of the spirits shobhad-neh revere can be seen in the magic of their religious leaders, and the skill and longevity of their snipers is a revered tradition across the Pact Worlds, which hints at a grain of empirical truth to the idea.

In addition to their traditional beliefs, many modern shobhads choose to worship gods, with Weydan a notable favorite. Those shobhads who live in large urban centers, were not raised in a clan, or have left behind their traditional way of life display a wider diversity in religions, favoring Besmara, Damoritosh, the Devourer, Elotiru, Ibra, Lao Shu Po, or even Zon-Shelyn.

Popular Edicts

master the skills necessary for survival so that you do not become a burden to others, show reverence for the world that gave you life

Popular Anathema

disrespect Akiton or another planet you call home through exploitative abuse of its resources, take more than you give to a community

Shobhad Mechanics

Hit Points 8

Size Large

Speed 25 Feet

Attribute Boosts Strength, Wisdom, Free

Attribute Flaw Charisma

Languages Akitonian, Common, Shobhad

Additional languages One regional language of your choice. Additional languages equal to your Intelligence modifier (if it's positive). Choose from the list of common languages and any other languages to which you have access (such as the languages prevalent on your home world).

Traits

  • humanoid, shobhad

Cold-Adapted

Vlaka.name: 弗拉卡 Vlaka
Vlaka.text:
弗拉卡人最初来自拉约克——一颗环绕着垂死恒星运行的严寒世界——为了生存，他们成为了星际游牧者。如今，拉约克的太阳奇迹般地重新燃起，在那些沐浴其光辉而生的弗拉卡人体内激发出奇异的太阳力量。

弗拉卡人以沉思、性情平和和善解人意而闻名。尽管他们曾长久地注视着自己世界不可避免的死亡，他们依然保持积极的态度与开放的心胸。他们虽非自我牺牲之辈，却愿意竭尽所能帮助家人、朋友和同伴，而且往往更强调群体的福祉而非个体的福祉。

尽管所有弗拉卡人都拥有敏锐的嗅觉与触觉，但他们的其他感官差异极大。失聪、失明以及又聋又盲的弗拉卡人都很常见。弗拉卡人善于解读情绪与社交信号，也很容易与他人结交朋友。

如果你想扮演一位胸襟开阔、心怀善意，并努力在银河各处行善的旅行者，那么你就该选择弗拉卡人。

你可能会……

  • 通过气味辨认他人及其情绪。
  • 听得比说得多。
  • 特意花额外心力去帮助别人。

他人可能会……

  • 依赖你提供指引和调解。
  • 认为只要开口请求，你就会答应帮助他们。
  • 试图利用你的善良、慷慨或乐于助人。

外貌描述

弗拉卡人看起来类似双足行走的狼，从他们蓬松摇摆的尾巴到犬类般的口鼻与牙齿皆是如此。他们拥有厚实的毛皮，使他们能在母星拉约克的极地气候中保持温暖；而对于许多以寒凉星舰走廊为家的游牧弗拉卡人来说，这层毛皮同样十分适用。他们的毛色通常是白色，并带有大片浅蓝、灰色或黑色斑块。许多个体在温暖气候中繁衍数代后会褪去这种厚毛，长出更为平滑的夏季毛皮，颜色多偏向白色或乳白色，并带有浅棕色斑块。弗拉卡人的眼睛通常为蓝色、灰色或白色。

弗拉卡人身材高大而健壮，平均身高约为5-1/2到6-1/2英尺，体重在175到250磅之间。几乎所有弗拉卡人共有的两种感官是触觉和极其敏锐的嗅觉。超过三分之二的弗拉卡人生来失明、失聪，或又聋又盲，其余则生来具有听觉与视觉。弗拉卡人将气味视为一种关键的非语言交流形式，并将气味融入从日常物品与地点到社交互动和娱乐活动在内的一切事物之中。

弗拉卡人嗅觉的强度与精确度（以及他们解读嗅觉输入的能力）因个体而异。大多数人会学着通过气味识别熟悉的人和地点，并能追踪浓烈的气味；而天赋异禀者甚至能够通过信息素的变化察觉情绪，乃至凭借气味感知周围环境。

社群

弗拉卡人重视倾听、共情与理解的能力。他们认为不耐烦、妄下结论和自私都是幼稚的特质，是那些尚未学会更好行事之人的表现。同样地，他们也强烈反感过度自傲、喧闹张扬、暗中使诈、贪婪或渴求权力。弗拉卡人强调——但并不总是优先考虑——群体的福祉高于个体的福祉。他们乐于奉献自己的时间与财富，常常不辞辛劳地帮助他人。

弗拉卡人的政府往往也秉持同样的价值观，尽职尽责地为社群的利益工作，并忠实地为其选民的意愿发声。领导是一项职责，近似于承担一种服务一生的生活，而非通往权力或名望的途径。

弗拉卡人的聚居地往往呈同心圆式且布局规整。外围环绕城市边界的一圈高大建筑或城墙，既能抵御寒冷、强风和恶劣天气，也能保存热量。最内层、也最受庇护的一圈，则保留给农业和公园等公共绿地。城市的其余部分会按兴趣领域或职业划分为环区或扇区并加以排列，例如娱乐、科学、治理或居住区。每个区域都有独特的气味和带有纹理的路面，使来自任何地方、拥有任何感官组合的弗拉卡人都能判断自己在城市中的大致位置。当某一区域满员时，城市往往会向下扩建，而不是突破区域边界。

示例名称

Aeru, Alaza, Alelell, Aufenren, Bargai, Berilt, Berkal, Breyul, Carok, Daran, Delevana, Jikant, Jilavo, Kalvek, Kapleth, Lycos, Metzari, Parva, Poskari, Renvir, Ritta, Tarnat, Torrik, Vaudil, Wirath, Wirgara, Yormin

信仰

传统的弗拉卡人民间故事与传说颂扬利他、英勇和慈善之举。他们没有祖先神；相反，他们传统上遵循死亡与重生的循环而生活，许多人也将这一循环融入自己的信仰之中。此外，弗拉卡人往往崇拜那些与他们以社群为中心的价值观相契合的神祇，例如海拉丝、兰巴图因和塔拉维特。游牧的弗拉卡人常崇拜Weydan，而那些致力于拯救自己星球，或对索塔重新燃起一事感到惊叹的人，则偏好与其所选方法论一致的神祇，例如Ibra、Eloritu或Yaraesa。有些人则崇拜像Desna和塞恩蕾这样的恒星神祇。

流行典范

在做出重要决定前考虑其他观点与意见，拥抱多样性，对待其

流行禁忌

将自己的欲望置于他人的需求之上，拒绝妥协，压制不同观点

弗拉卡机制

生命值 6

体型 中型

速度 25英尺

属性增强 感知、魅力、自由

属性缺陷 敏捷

语言 通用语、弗拉卡语、你选择的一种地区语言

额外语言 等于你的智力调整值（如果其为正）。从通用语言列表以及你能够接触到的任何其他语言中选择（例如你母星上通行的语言）。

特征

  • 人形生物

Originally from Lajok, a frigid world orbiting a dying sun, vlakas became stellar nomads to survive. Now, Lajok's sun has miraculously rekindled, sparking strange solar powers within vlakas born under its light.

Vlakas have a reputation for being contemplative, even-tempered, and understanding. Despite long ages spent watching the inevitable death of their world, they keep a positive outlook and an open mind. Though not self-sacrificing, they're willing to go to great lengths to help their family, friends, and colleagues, and they tend to emphasize the well- being of the group over that of the individual.

While all vlakas share a keen sense of smell and touch, their other senses vary wildly. Deaf, blind, and deafblind vlakas are all born frequently. Vlakas are good at reading emotions and social cues, and they find it easy to make friends with others.

If you want to play an open-minded and well-intentioned traveler who strives to do good throughout the galaxy, you should play a vlaka.

You Might...

  • Identify people and their emotions by scent.
  • Listen more than speak.
  • Go out of your way to help others.

Others Probably...

  • Depend on you for guidance and mediation.
  • Assume you'll agree to help them whenever they ask.
  • Try to exploit your kindness, generosity, or helpfulness.

Physical Description

Vlakas look similar to bipedal wolves, from their bushy, wagging tail to their canine snout and teeth. They have thick fur that keeps them warm in the arctic climate of their home world Lajok, which serves them just as well in the cool starship corridors that many nomadic vlakas call home. Their fur is usually white with large patches of pale blue, gray, or black. Many shed it after spending generations in warmer climates and grow a sleek summer coat that leans toward shades of white or cream with pale brown patches. Vlakas usually have blue, gray, or white eyes.

Vlakas are tall and athletic, averaging between 5-1/2 to 6-1/2 feet tall and 175 to 250 lbs. The two senses that nearly all vlakas share are touch and an incredibly keen sense of smell. More than two thirds of all vlakas are born blind, deaf, or deafblind, with the remainder born hearing/sighted. Vlakas consider scent a crucial form of non-verbal communication and infuse smells into everything from everyday objects and locations to social interactions and entertainment.

The strength and precision of a vlaka's sense of smell (and their ability to interpret olfactory inputs) varies between individuals. While most learn to identify familiar people and places by scent and can track a potent scent, the truly gifted can detect emotions by changes in pheromones and even sense their surroundings by smell.

Society

Vlakas value the ability to listen, empathize, and understand. They consider impatience, jumping to conclusions, and selfishness all childish traits the acts of those who have yet to learn better. Likewise, they strongly frown upon being overly proud, boisterous, underhanded, greedy, or power hungry. Vlakas emphasize, but not always prioritize,the well-being of the group over that of the individual. They're charitable with their time and wealth, often going out of their way to aid others.

Vlaka governments tend to uphold these same values, dutifully working for their community's benefit and faithfully advocating for the will of their constituents. Leadership is a duty akin to taking on a life of service, not a route to power or fame.

Vlaka settlements tend to be concentric and formulaic. An outer ring of tall buildings or walls around the perimeter protects against the cold, wind, and bad weather as well as conserves heat. The innermost ring, where it's the most sheltered, is reserved for agriculture and public green spaces like parks. The rest of the city is divided into rings or wedges and arranged by area of interest or profession, such as entertainment, science, governance, or residential. Each district has a distinct scent and textured pavement, enabling vlakas from anywhere and with any array of senses to determine their general location within a city. When a district is full, the city tends to build down, rather than broach district boundaries.

Sample Names

Aeru, Alaza, Alelell, Aufenren, Bargai, Berilt, Berkal, Breyul, Carok, Daran, Delevana, Jikant, Jilavo, Kalvek, Kapleth, Lycos, Metzari, Parva, Poskari, Renvir, Ritta, Tarnat, Torrik, Vaudil, Wirath, Wirgara, Yormin

Beliefs

Traditional vlaka folktales and legends extol altruism, heroism, and charitable acts. They have no ancestral gods; instead, they traditionally live by the Cycle of death and rebirth, and many incorporate the Cycle into their beliefs. Otherwise, vlakas tend to worship gods who share their community-centered values, such as Hylax, Lambatuin, and Talavet. Nomadic vlakas often worship Weydan, while those who strive to save their planet or who wonder at Sota's rekindling favor gods who share their methodologies of choice, such as Ibra, Eloritu, or Yaraesa. Some worship stellar gods like Desna and Sarenrae.

Popular Edicts

consider other viewpoints and opinions before making important decisions, embrace diversity, treat oth

Popular Anathema

put your desires above others' needs, refuse to compromise, suppress alternative viewpoints

Vlaka Mechanics

Hit Points 6

Size Medium

Speed 25 Feet

Attribute Boosts Wisdom, Charisma, Free

Attribute Flaw Dexterity

Languages Common, Vlaka, Regional language of your choice

Additional languages equal to your Intelligence modifier (if it's positive). Choose from the list of common languages and any other languages to which you have access (such as the languages prevalent on your home world).

Traits

  • humanoid
  • vlaka

Bonuses and Penalties.name: 加值和减值 Bonuses and Penalties
Bonuses and Penalties.text:
加值

如果你拥有会适用于同一次掷骰的不同类型加值，你会将它们全部加上。但如果你拥有多个同类型加值，则一次掷骰中只能使用其中最高的加值——它们不会累加。

熟练加值

熟练等级熟练加值
未受训0
受训你的等级 + 2
专家你的等级 + 4
大师你的等级 + 6
传奇你的等级 + 8

境况加值

与您在尝试检定时所处的情况有关。

物品加值

由你正在穿戴或使用的某件物品给予，无论是普通物品还是魔法物品。

状态加值

通常来自法术、其他魔法效果，或某种施加在你身上的有益且通常为暂时性的状态。

减值

减值的运作方式与加值非常相似。你可能会有境况减值、状态减值，有时甚至还有物品减值。与同类型加值相同，对于同一类型的多个减值，你只承受其中最严重的那个。

章节：进行游戏《Starfinder 玩家核心》 第392页

Bonuses

If you have different types of bonuses that would apply to the same roll, you'll add them all. But if you have multiple bonuses of the same type, you can use only the highest bonus on a given roll—they aren't cumulative.

Proficiency bonus

Proficiency RankProficiency Bonus
Untrained0
TrainedYour level + 2
ExpertYour level + 4
MasterYour level + 6
LegendaryYour level + 8

Circumstance bonuses

Involve the situation you find yourself in when attempting a check.

Item bonuses

Are granted by some item that you are wearing or using, either mundane or magical.

Status bonuses

Typically come from spells, other magical effects, or something applying a helpful, often temporary, condition to you.

Penalties

Penalties work very much like bonuses. You can have circumstance penalties, status penalties, and sometimes even item penalties. Like bonuses of the same type, you take only the worst all of various penalties of a given type.

Section: Playing the GameStarfinder Player Core pg. 392

Death, Dying and Unconscious.name: 死亡、濒死和失去意识 Death, Dying and Unconscious
Death, Dying and Unconscious.text:
死亡与濒死

被击倒当你的 HP 降至 0 时，将你的先攻顺序移动到使你 HP 降至 0 的生物或效果之前的紧邻位置。获得濒死 1；如果伤害来自重击，或你在一次豁免中重度失败，则改为获得濒死 2。非致命效果会使你在 0 HP 时陷入 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{失去意识}，且不会使你获得濒死状态。
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yZRUzMqrMmfLu0V1]{濒死}

你处于 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{失去意识}。如果你的濒死值达到 4，你就会死亡。在你的回合开始时进行一次恢复检定，以决定你的状况是好转还是恶化。如果你在任何时候拥有 1 点或更多 HP，你就会失去濒死状态。每当你失去濒死状态时，你的受伤值增加 1。当你处于濒死时受到伤害，濒死值增加 1（若敌人重度成功，或你重度失败，则增加 2）。

恢复检定

当你处于濒死状态时，在你的回合开始时进行一次平检（DC 10 + 你的濒死值）。

重度成功 你的濒死值减少 2。

成功 你的濒死值减少 1。

失败 你的濒死值增加 1。

重度失败 你的濒死值增加 2

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2]{受伤}如果你在拥有受伤状态时获得濒死状态，将你的受伤值加到你的濒死值上。若你通过 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{治疗伤势} 恢复 HP，或者你的 HP 恢复至全满并休息 10 分钟，则受伤结束。
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{厄运}你会死亡的最大濒死值会因你的厄运值而降低。如果你的最大濒死值被降至 0，你会立即死亡。每当你获得一整晚充分休息后，你的厄运值减少 1。

失去意识

当你的生命值为 0 时，你无法从失去意识中醒来。如果你处于失去意识且拥有 1 点或更多生命值，你会以下列五种方式之一醒来。

  • 你受到伤害，前提是该伤害不会使你降至 0 HP。
  • 你接受了治疗，但休息带来的自然治疗除外。
  • 有人以一个交互动作轻推或摇晃你，把你弄醒。
  • 如果有巨大噪音，则在你的回合开始时对该噪音的 DC 进行一次察觉检定；若成功，你就会醒来。如果有生物正试图保持安静，则改为使用它们的潜行 DC。

  • GM 决定你是否会醒来：要么是因为你经历了一整晚安稳的睡眠，要么是因为某些事打断了这场安稳的睡眠。

章节：进行游戏Starfinder Player Core 第 435-441 页

Death and Dying

Knocked OutWhen reduced to 0 HP, move your initiative to directly before the creature or effect that reduced you to 0 HP. Gain dying 1, or dying 2 if the damage came from a critical hit or your critical failure on a save. A nonlethal effect makes you @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious} at 0 HP and doesn't give you the dying condition.
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.yZRUzMqrMmfLu0V1]{Dying}

You are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.fBnFDH2MTzgFijKf]{Unconscious}. If you ever reach dying 4, you die. Attempt a recovery check at the start of your turn to determine whether you get better or worse. If you ever have 1 HP or more, you lose the dying condition. Any time you lose the dying condition, increase your wounded value by 1. If you take damage while dying, increase the dying value by 1 (or 2 on an enemy's critical success or your critical failure).

Recovery Checks

At the start of your turn when you're dying, attempt a flat check (DC 10 + your dying value).

Critical Success Your dying value is reduced by 2.

Success Your dying value is reduced by 1.

Failure Your dying value increases by 1.

Critical Failure Your dying value increases by 2

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Yl48xTdMh3aeQYL2]{Wounded}If you gain the dying condition while you have the wounded condition, add your wounded value to your dying value. Wounded ends if you receive HP from @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.1kGNdIIhuglAjIp9]{Treat Wounds}, or if you're restored to full HP and rest for 10 minutes.
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.3uh1r86TzbQvosxv]{Doomed}The maximum dying value at which you die is reduced by your doomed value. If your maximum dying value is reduced to 0, you instantly die. Your doomed value decreases by 1 each time you get a full night's rest.

Unconscious

You can't wake up from Unconsciousness while you have 0 Hit Points. If you're unconscious and have 1 or more Hit Points, you wake up in one of five ways.

  • You take damage, provided the damage doesn't drop you to 0 HP.
  • You receive healing, other than natural healing from resting.
  • Someone nudges or shakes you awake with an Interact action.
  • If there's loud noise, at the start of your turn attempt a Perception check against the noise's DC, waking up if you succeed. If creatures are attempting to stay quiet, this uses their Stealth DC.

  • The GM decides you wake up either because you have had a restful night's sleep or something disrupted that restful sleep.

Section: Playing the GameStarfinder Player Core pg. 435-441

Basic Actions.name: 基本动作 Basic Actions
Basic Actions.text:
动作特征描述
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.HCl3pzVefiv9ZKQW]{Aid} RDC 15 检定，给予受协助的技能检定或攻击检定 +1 环境加值（重击成功：+2，若为大师则 +3，若为传奇则 +4）。
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.oc0ZKnk5zD0GXqZi]{Area Fire} 2

区域

攻击

你以一个等于该武器射程增量的锥形或线形区域，或一个指定爆发半径的区域，命中区域内每个生物（基础反射豁免，DC 等于你的职业 DC 加上该武器的追踪值）。
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.qm7xptMSozAinnPS]{Arrest a Fall} R在飞行时使用特技来减缓你的坠落。
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.6ctkxUnVwqooSvgg]{Auto-Fire} 2

区域

攻击

你命中一个锥形区域内的每个生物，该区域等于该武器射程增量的一半（基础反射豁免，DC 等于你的职业 DC 加上该武器的追踪值）。这会消耗等于区域内目标数量 × 2 的弹药。
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.UWdRX1VelipCzrCg]{Avert Gaze} 1对抗视觉能力时获得 +2 环境加值。
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.H6v1VgowHaKHnVlG]{Burrow} 1

说话

只要你能够行动，你也可以说话。你不需要花费任何类型的动作来说话，但由于一轮代表 6 秒时间，所以你通常每轮至多只能说大约一句话。言语的特殊用法，例如尝试进行一次欺瞒技能检定来撒谎，需要花费动作并遵循其自身规则。所有言语都具有听觉特征。如果你以言语以外的某种方式交流，则可能适用其他规则。举例来说，使用手语具有视觉而非听觉特征。

章节：进行游戏《Starfinder Player Core》 第 408-411 页

Speaking

As long as you can act, you can also speak. You don't need to spend any type of action to speak, but because a round represents 6 seconds of time, you can usually speak at most a single sentence or so per round. Special uses of speech, such as attempting a Deception skill check to Lie, require spending actions and follow their own rules. All speech has the auditory trait. If you communicate in some way other than speech, other rules might apply. For instance, using sign language is visual instead of auditory.

Section: Playing the GameStarfinder Player Core pg. 408-411

Terrain and Cover.name: 地形与掩蔽 Terrain and Cover
Terrain and Cover.text:
地形

困难地形每个方格需要额外 5 尺移动距离。
更严重困难地形每个方格需要额外 10 尺移动距离。
危险地形穿过危险地形会受到伤害。
狭窄表面处于措手不及，且必须进行平衡才能通过。当你被击中或豁免失败时，必须成功通过一次反射豁免，否则坠落。
不平整地面处于措手不及，并且可能需要进行平衡，否则倒地。当你被击中或豁免失败时，必须成功通过一次反射豁免，否则坠落。
斜坡你需要进行攀爬才能向上通过斜坡。攀爬时你处于措手不及。

掩护

从攻击者所占空间或爆发区域的中心，画一条线到目标所占空间的中心。

掩护类型加值能否躲藏
较少掩护如果连线穿过生物而未穿过物体，则 AC 获得 +1 环境加值。否
掩护（标准）AC、对抗区域效果的反射豁免，以及用于躲藏或潜行的隐匿检定获得 +2 环境加值。你可以使用 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.ust1jJSCZQUhBZIz]{利用掩护} 将其提升为更高级掩护。可以
更高级掩护如同掩护，但加值为 +4。可以

章节：进行游戏《Starfinder 玩家核心》pg. 415-416

Terrain

Difficult TerrainEach square costs 5 extra feet of movement.
Greater Difficult TerrainEach square costs 10 extra feet of movement.
Hazardous TerrainMoving through hazardous terrain deals damage.
Narrow SurfaceOff-guard and must Balance to cross. When you're hit or fail a save, succeed at a Reflex save or fall.
Uneven GroundOff-guard and might need to Balance or fall prone. When you're hit or fail a save, succeed at a Reflex save or fall.
InclineYou need to Climb to ascend an incline. You're off-guard while Climbing.

Cover

Draw a line from the center of the attacker's space or burst to the center of the target's space.

Type of CoverBonusCan Hide
Lesser Cover+1 circumstance bonus to AC if line passes through creatures but no objects.No
Cover (Standard)+2 circumstance bonus to AC, Reflex saves against area effects, and Stealth checks to Hide or Sneak. You can use @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.ust1jJSCZQUhBZIz]{Take Cover} to increase this to greater cover.Yes
Greater CoverAs cover, but a +4 bonus.Yes

Section: Playing the GameStarfinder Player Core pg. 415-416

Falling.name: 坠落 Falling
Falling.text:
当你坠落超过5英尺时，受到等于你坠落距离一半的钝击伤害。如果你受到任何伤害，你将以 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{俯卧} 状态落地。如果你坠入水中或柔软物质中，则按坠落距离缩短20英尺来计算伤害；如果你是有意跳入，则缩短30英尺（最多不超过该物质的深度）。

坠落到生物身上

如果你落在一个生物身上，该生物必须尝试一次 @Check[reflex|dc:15] 豁免。在长距离坠落后有意瞄准自己落到某个生物身上几乎是不可能的。

大成功 该生物不受伤害。

成功 该生物受到等于你所受坠落伤害四分之一的钝击伤害。

失败 该生物受到等于你所受坠落伤害一半的钝击伤害。

大失败 该生物受到与你因坠落所受伤害相同数值的钝击伤害。

坠落物体

掉落的物体受到伤害的方式与坠落生物相同。如果该物体落在一个生物身上，该生物可以按照生物坠落到生物身上的相同规则尝试一次反射豁免。涉及坠落物体的危险和法术，例如岩崩，对于它们如何与生物互动以及造成何种伤害，都有各自的规则。

章节：进行游戏《Starfinder 玩家核心》 第413页

When you fall more than 5 feet, take bludgeoning damage equal to half the distance you fell. If you take any damage, you land @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}. If you fall into water or a soft substance, calculate damage as though the fall were 20 feet shorter, 30 if you intentionally dove in (up to the depth of the substance).

Falling on a Creature

If you land on a creature, that creature must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:15] save. Intentionally aiming yourself to land on a creature after a long fall is almost impossible.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes bludgeoning damage equal to one-quarter the falling damage you took.

Failure The creature takes bludgeoning damage equal to half the falling damage you took.

Critical Failure The creature takes the same amount of bludgeoning damage you took from the fall.

Falling Objects

A dropped object takes damage just like a falling creature. If the object lands on a creature, that creature can attempt a Reflex save using the same rules as for a creature falling on a creature. Hazards and spells that involve falling objects, such as a rock slide, have their own rules about how they interact with creatures and the damage they deal.

Section: Playing the GameStarfinder Player Core pg. 413

你被下达了命令、受到魔法支配，或以其他方式意志遭到颠覆。控制者决定你如何行动，并且可以让你使用你的任意动作，包括攻击、反应，甚至 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.A72nHGUtNXgY5Ey9]{Delay}。控制者在控制你时通常不需要花费他们自己的动作。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching}

具有科技特征且处于故障状态的生物、危害或物体，会经历一系列削弱性效果以及不时卡顿停滞的情况。故障状态总是带有一个状态值。如果你的装备处于故障状态，并且你采取任何涉及该装备的动作，你必须进行一次平检来确定会发生什么。如果你具有故障状态，你必须在你的每个回合开始时尝试此 @Check[flat|dc:resolve(5 + @item.badge.value)|showDC:all]。该平检的 DC 等于 5 + 你的状态值，或该物品的状态值。

大成功 你的故障状态值降低 1。

成功 你如常行动，或如常使用你的装备。

失败 你所有检定和 DC 都承受等于你的故障状态值，或你正尝试使用之物品的故障状态值的物品减值，这包括豁免检定、攻击调整值，以及你使用的能力、效果和法术的 DC。

大失败 你尝试使用的故障物体无法运作，而你失去为尝试使用它所花费的动作。处于故障状态的生物承受与失败相同的效果，且还会 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}。处于故障状态的危害承受与失败相同的效果。

你正在承受来自持续性效果的伤害，例如被火点燃时。这会显示为“X 点持续[type]伤害”，其中“X”是造成的伤害量，而“[type]”是伤害类型。与通常的伤害一样，它可以根据攻击检定或豁免检定的结果加倍或减半。你不会立刻承受持续伤害，而是在你具有该状态期间，于你的每个回合结束时承受一次，并且每次都重新投掷所有伤害骰。在你承受持续伤害后，进行一次 @Check[flat|showDC:all|dc:15] 以查看你是否从持续伤害中恢复。如果你成功，该状态结束。

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.enA7BxAjBb7ns1iF]{Suppressed}你受到了大量来袭火力或某次特别危险攻击的影响，为了自身安全而不得不以较低效率行动。你的攻击检定承受 –1 情况减值，且你的所有速度承受 –10 尺状态减值。
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.z1ucw4CLwLqHoAp3]{Untethered}你处于零重力（或类似）环境中，且没有移动手段，只能在没有支撑的情况下漂浮。你不能采取移动动作，除非该动作明确说明可在你当前环境中使用。在你的回合结束时，你会沿着你上次移动的方向移动 5 尺。你可以采取 Push Off 动作来改变方向和移动距离。一旦你获得了在该环境中移动的手段，你便失去该状态。通常，在零重力下具有未束缚的状态的生物，在未束缚时还会获得 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} 和 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} 状态。

章节：状态附录《Starfinder 玩家核心》pg. 435-441

You have been commanded, magically dominated, or otherwise had your will subverted. The controller dictates how you act and can make you use any of your actions, including attacks, reactions, or even @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.A72nHGUtNXgY5Ey9]{Delay}. The controller usually doesn't have to spend their own actions when controlling you.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.6A2QDy8wRGCVQsSd]{Glitching}

A glitching creature, hazard, or object with the tech trait experiences a combination of debilitating effects and moments of seizing up. The glitching condition always includes a value. If you have glitching equipment and take any action involving that equipment, you must attempt a flat check to see what occurs. If you have the glitching condition, you must attempt this @Check[flat|dc:resolve(5 + @item.badge.value)|showDC:all] at the beginning of each of your turns. The DC of the flat check equals 5 plus your condition value or the item's condition value.

Critical Success Reduce your glitching value by 1.

Success You act as normal or use your equipment as normal.

Failure You take an item penalty to all your checks and DCs equal to your glitching value or the glitching value of the item you're attempting to use, including saving throws, attack modifiers, and the DCs of abilities, effects, and spells you use.

Critical Failure A glitching object you tried to use doesn't function, and you lose the actions you took to attempt to use it. A glitching creature uses the same effect as a failure and is also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.dfCMdR4wnpbYNTix]{Stunned 1}. A glitching hazard uses the same effect as a failure.

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.lDVqvLKA6eF3Df60]{Persistent Damage}

You are taking damage from an ongoing effect, such as from being lit on fire. This appears as "X persistent [type] damage," where "X" is the amount of damage dealt and "[type]" is the damage type. Like normal damage, it can be doubled or halved based on the results of an attack roll or saving throw. Instead of taking persistent damage immediately, you take it at the end of each of your turns as long as you have the condition, rolling any damage dice anew each time. After you take persistent damage, roll a @Check[flat|showDC:all|dc:15] to see if you recover from the persistent damage. If you succeed, the condition ends.

You take a –1 circumstance penalty to attack rolls and a –10-foot status penalty to all your Speeds.
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.z1ucw4CLwLqHoAp3]{Untethered}You're in a zero gravity (or similar) environment without a means of movement and float without support. You can't take move actions unless they specify they can be used in your current environment. At the end of your turn, you move 5 feet in the last direction you moved. You can take the Push Off action to change directions and the distance moved. Once you gain a means of moving in your environment, you lose this condition. Typically, a creature with the untethered condition in zero-gravity also gains the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} conditions while untethered.

Section: Conditions AppendixStarfinder Player Core pg. 435-441

Counteracting.name: 反制 Counteracting
Counteracting.text:
某些效果会尝试反制法术、负面状态、状态或其他效果。反制检定会比较两种力量的强弱，并决定哪一方压倒另一方。成功反制一个效果会扰乱它，使其无法产生任何效果，除非另有说明。

当你尝试进行一次反制检定时，将相关技能调整值或其他适用的调整值加入你的检定，对抗目标的DC。若你在反制一个负面状态，DC会写在该负面状态的数据栏中。若它是一个法术，则使用施法者的DC。GM也可以根据目标效果的等级来计算DC。对于法术，反制检定的调整值等于你的施法属性调整值加上你的法术施法熟练加值，再加上任何明确适用于反制检定的加值与减值。

你能反制什么，取决于检定结果以及目标的反制环阶。若一个效果是法术，则其环阶就是反制环阶。否则，将其等级减半并向上取整，以决定其反制环阶（最低反制环阶为0）。若一个效果的等级不明确且它来自一个生物，则将该生物的等级减半并向上取整。

 大成功 若目标的反制环阶至多比你的效果的反制环阶高3，则反制该目标。

 成功 若目标的反制环阶至多比你的效果的反制环阶高1，则反制该目标。

 失败 若目标的反制环阶低于你的效果的反制环阶，则反制该目标。

 大失败 你未能反制目标。

反制表

反制环阶失败成功大成功

10 (-1 to 0)2 (3 to 4)4 (7 to 8)
21 (1 to 2)3 (5 to 6)5 (9 to 10)
32 (3 to 4)4 (7 to 8)6 (11 to 12)
43 (5 to 6)5 (9 to 10)7 (13 to 14)
54 (7 to 8)6 (11 to 12)8 (15 to 16)
65 (9 to 10)7 (13 to 14)9 (17 to 18)
76 (11 to 12)8 (15 to 16)10 (19 to 20)
87 (13 to 14)9 (17 to 18)11 (21 to 22)
98 (15 to 16)10 (19 to 20)12 (23 to 24)
109 (17 to 18)11 (21 to 22)13 (25 to 26)

每一列中的第一个数字，表示你根据自己的成功等级可以反制效果时所对应的反制环阶。括号中的数字则是与该环阶对应的典型等级范围。

章节：进行游戏《星际探险者玩家核心》 第423页

Some effects try to counteract spells, afflictions, conditions, or other effects. Counteract checks compare the power of two forces and determine which defeats the other. Successfully counteracting an effect disrupts it, preventing it from having any effect, unless noted otherwise.

When attempting a counteract check, add the relevant skill modifier or other appropriate modifier to your check against the target's DC. If you're counteracting an affliction, the DC is in the affliction's stat block. If it's a spell, use the caster's DC. The GM can also calculate a DC based on the target effect's level. For spells, the counteract check modifier is your spellcasting attribute modifier plus your spellcasting proficiency bonus, plus any bonuses and penalties that specifically apply to counteract checks.

What you can counteract depends on the check result and the target's counteract rank. If an effect is a spell, its rank is the counteract rank. Otherwise, halve its level and round up to determine its counteract rank (minimum counteract rank 0). If an effect's level is unclear and it came from a creature, halve and round up the creature's level.

 Critical Success Counteract the target if its counteract rank is no more than 3 higher than your effect's counteract rank.

 Success Counteract the target if its counteract rank is no more than 1 higher than your effect's counteract rank.

 Failure Counteract the target if its counteract rank is lower than your effect's counteract rank.

 Critical Failure You fail to counteract the target.

Counteract Table

Counteract RankFailureSuccessCritical Success
0—1 (1 to 2)3 (5 to 6)
10 (-1 to 0)2 (3 to 4)4 (7 to 8)
21 (1 to 2)3 (5 to 6)5 (9 to 10)
32 (3 to 4)4 (7 to 8)6 (11 to 12)
43 (5 to 6)5 (9 to 10)7 (13 to 14)
54 (7 to 8)6 (11 to 12)8 (15 to 16)
65 (9 to 10)7 (13 to 14)9 (17 to 18)
76 (11 to 12)8 (15 to 16)10 (19 to 20)

109 (17 to 18)11 (21 to 22)13 (25 to 26)

The first number in each column is the counteract rank at which you can counteract an effect based on your degree of success. The numbers in parentheses are the typical level range corresponding to that rank.

Section: Playing the GameStarfinder Player Core pg. 423

Light.name: 光照 Light
Light.text:
光照等级描述
明亮光照在明亮光照下，例如阳光中，任何拥有普通视力或更好视力的人都能清楚地观察到生物和物体。
微光处于阴影中或由微弱光源照亮的区域为微光。处于微光中的生物和物体具有 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{隐蔽} 状况，除非搜索者拥有黑暗视觉、昏暗视觉，或视觉以外的精确感官。
黑暗处于黑暗中的生物或物体为 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{隐匿} 或未被察觉，除非搜索者拥有黑暗视觉或视觉以外的精确感官。没有黑暗视觉或其他在黑暗中感知手段的生物在黑暗中具有 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{目盲} 状况，尽管它仍可能看见黑暗之外被照亮的区域。如果一个生物能够看见被照亮的区域，它就能正常观察该照亮区域中的生物。在身处黑暗之后，突然暴露在明亮光照下可能会让你在短时间内 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{目眩}，具体由GM决定。

章节：进行游戏《Starfinder Player Core》 第424页

Levels of LightDescription
Bright LightIn bright light, such as sunlight, creatures and objects can be observed clearly by anyone with average vision or better.
Dim LightAreas in shadow or lit by weak light sources are in dim light. Creatures and objects in dim light have the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} condition, unless the seeker has darkvision or low-light vision, or a precise sense other than vision.
DarknessA creature or object within darkness is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} or undetected unless the seeker has darkvision or a precise sense other than vision. A creature without darkvision or another means of perceiving in darkness has the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} condition while in darkness, though it might be able to see illuminated areas beyond the darkness. If a creature can see into an illuminated area, it can observe creatures within that illuminated area normally. After being in darkness, sudden exposure to bright light might make you @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for a short time, as determined by the GM.

Section: Playing the GameStarfinder Player Core pg. 424

Senses.name: 感官 Senses
Senses.text:
生物如何使用察觉，取决于它拥有哪些感官。

精确感官

正常视觉是一种精确感官——一种能够以细致入微的方式感知世界的感官。想要在没有任何不利影响的情况下以生物为目标，唯一的方法就是使用精确感官。你通常可以用精确感官自动觉察到一个生物，除非该生物正在躲藏或被环境遮蔽；在这种情况下，你可以使用 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{搜索} 基础动作来更好地发现该生物。

不精确感官

正常听觉是一种不精确感官——它无法探知精确感官所能探知的全部细节。你通常可以用不精确感官自动感觉到一个生物，但该生物获得的是 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{隐蔽} 状况，而非被观测到状况。如果它正在使用隐匿，或处于会干扰这种感官的环境中，例如对听觉而言的嘈杂房间，那么它对你来说可能是未被察觉的。在这些情况下，你必须使用 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{搜索} 基础动作来发现该生物。在最佳情况下，不精确感官可以将一个未被察觉的生物（或一个你甚至不知道在那里存在的生物）变为仅仅是隐蔽——它无法让该生物变为被观测到。

模糊感官

角色还有许多模糊感官——它们能提醒你某种东西在那里，但不足以帮助你锁定它并准确判断它究竟是什么。对于典型角色而言，其中最有用的通常是嗅觉。在最佳情况下，模糊感官可用于觉察一个未被注意到的生物的存在，使其变为未被察觉。即便如此，模糊感官也不足以让该生物成为 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{隐蔽} 或被观测到。

特殊感官

黑暗视觉与高等黑暗视觉

拥有黑暗视觉或高等黑暗视觉的生物，能够在黑暗和微光区域中看得一清二楚，不过这种视觉只能呈现黑白颜色。某些形式的魔法黑暗，例如 4环 darkness 法术，会阻挡普通黑暗视觉。然而，拥有高等黑暗视觉的生物甚至能看穿这些形式的魔法黑暗。

微光视觉

拥有微光视觉的生物在微光中视物如同处于明亮光照下，因此它会忽略由微光导致的 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{隐蔽} 状况。

嗅觉

嗅觉是通过气味来感知生物或物体，通常属于一种模糊感官。其范围会列在该能力中，而且只有当被探测的生物或物体会散发气味时才能生效。如果一个生物散发出浓烈气味，或位于上风处，GM 可以将用于探测该生物的嗅觉能力范围加倍，甚至增加到三倍；如果一个生物位于下风处，GM 也可以缩短该范围。

震动感知

震动感知让生物能够感受到移动通过固体表面引起的振动。它通常是一种具有有限范围（列在该能力中）的不精确感官。只有当探测者与目标位于同一表面上，且目标正在沿着该表面移动（或掘穿该表面）时，震动感知才会生效。

章节：进行游戏Starfinder Player Core 第424-425页

The ways a creature can use Perception depend on what senses it has.

Precise Senses

Average vision is a precise sense—a sense that can be used to perceive the world in nuanced detail. The only way to target a creature without having drawbacks is to use a precise sense. You can usually detect a creature automatically with a precise sense unless that creature is hiding or obscured by the environment, in which case you can use the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} basic action to better detect the creature.

Imprecise Senses

Average hearing is an imprecise sense—it can't detect the full range of detail that a precise sense can. You can usually sense a creature automatically with an imprecise sense, but it has the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} condition instead of the observed condition. It might be undetected by you if it's using Stealth or is in an environment that distorts the sense, such as a noisy room in the case of hearing. In those cases, you have to use the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} basic action to detect the creature. At best, an imprecise sense can be used to make an undetected creature (or one you didn't even know was there) merely hidden—it can't make the creature observed.

Vague Senses

A character also has many vague senses—ones that can alert you that something is there but aren't useful for zeroing in on it to determine exactly what it is. The most useful of these for a typical character is the sense of smell. At best, a vague sense can be used to detect the presence of an unnoticed creature, making it undetected. Even then, the vague sense isn't sufficient to make the creature @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} or observed.

Special Senses

Darkvision and Greater Darkvision

A creature with darkvision or greater darkvision can see perfectly well in areas of darkness and dim light, though such vision is in black and white only. Some forms of magical darkness, such as a 4th-rank darkness spell, block normal darkvision. A creature with greater darkvision, however, can see through even these forms of magical darkness.

Low-Light Vision

A creature with low-light vision can see in dim light as though it were bright light, so it ignores the @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} condition due to dim light.

Scent

Scent involves sensing creatures or objects by smell, and is usually a vague sense. The range is listed in the ability, and it functions only if the creature or object being detected emits an aroma. If a creature emits a heavy aroma or is upwind, the GM can double or even triple the range of scent abilities used to detect that creature, and the GM can reduce the range if a creature is downwind.

Tremorsense

Tremorsense allows a creature to feel the vibrations through a solid surface caused by movement. It is usually an imprecise sense with a limited range (listed in the ability). Tremorsense functions only if the detecting creature is on the same surface as the subject, and only if the subject is moving along (or burrowing through) the surface.

Section: Playing the GameStarfinder Player Core pg. 424-425

Detecting Creatures.name: 侦测生物 Detecting Creatures
Detecting Creatures.text:
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.1wQY3JYyhMYeeV2G]{Observed}观察到你的生物知道你在哪里，并且可以正常将你作为目标。
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}对被你隐蔽的生物而言，若以非区域效果将你作为目标，则必须通过一次 @Check[flat|dc:5]。
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden}对被你隐匿的生物而言，它知道你所在的空间。它对你处于 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard}，并且必须通过一次 @Check[flat|dc:11] 才能影响到你。你可以使用躲藏来变为隐匿，并用 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} 来寻找隐匿的生物。
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VRSef5y1LmL2Hkjf]{Undetected}当某个生物无法察觉你时，它对你处于 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard}，看不见你，不知道你占据哪个空间，也不能将你作为目标。它可以通过选择一个方格并尝试进行一次攻击来猜测你的位置。这如同以 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} 生物为目标，但平检和攻击检定都由GM秘密掷骰。
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.9evPzg9E6muFcoSk]{Unnoticed}对无法注意到你的生物而言，它并未察觉到你的存在。
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible}所有生物都无法察觉你。处于隐形时，除非通过特殊能力或魔法，否则你不能变为被观察到。

章节：进行游戏《Starfinder 玩家核心》 第425页

@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.1wQY3JYyhMYeeV2G]{Observed}A creature you're observed by knows where you are and can target you normally.
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed}A creature that you're concealed from must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:5] when targeting you with a non-area effect.
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden}A creature you're hidden from knows the space you're in. It is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to you, and must succeed at a @Check[flat|dc:11] to affect you. You can Hide to become hidden, and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.BlAOM2X92SI6HMtJ]{Seek} to find hidden creatures.
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.VRSef5y1LmL2Hkjf]{Undetected}When you are undetected by a creature, it's @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} to you, can't see you, has no idea what space you occupy, and can't target you. It can try to guess your square by picking a square and attempting an attack. This works like targeting a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} creature, but the flat check and attack roll are rolled in secret by the GM.
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.9evPzg9E6muFcoSk]{Unnoticed}A creature you're unnoticed by is unaware of your presence.
@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible}You're undetected by everyone. You can't become observed while invisible except via special abilities or magic.

Section: Playing the GameStarfinder Player Core pg. 425

Detecting and Stealth with Other Senses.name: 用其他感官侦测和隐秘 Detecting and Stealth with Other Senses
Detecting and Stealth with Other Senses.text:
以其他感官进行察觉

大多数指定“一个你能看见的生物”或类似目标的能力，如果使用者能通过另一种感官精确感知该对象，同样可以正常生效。若怪物使用视觉以外的感官，GM可以调整适用于该怪物感官的规避侦测方式。例如，拥有回声定位的生物可能会将听觉作为主要感官。这可能意味着，它的猎物在嘈杂的房间中处于@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{隐蔽}状态，在足够喧闹的环境中处于@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{隐匿}状态，或者在@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.gIdDLrbswTV3OBJy]{沉默术}之下处于@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{隐形}状态。

将潜行用于其他感官

潜行技能的设计中，Hide用于规避视觉侦测，而@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.IE2nThCmoyhQA0Jn]{避免注意}和@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.VMozDqMMuK5kpoX4]{潜行移动}则用于避免被看到和听到。对于许多特殊感官，玩家可以描述自己是如何规避该特殊感官的侦测，并使用最合适的潜行动作。例如，一个生物为了避免被震动感知察觉而轻手轻脚地移动时，就是在使用潜行移动。

在某些情况下，进行基于敏捷的潜行技能检定来潜行移动并不是最合理的做法。例如，一名PC试图避免被能够感知心跳的生物察觉时，可能会通过冥想来放慢心率，在其潜行检定中使用感知而非敏捷。当一个生物能够通过多种不同感官来侦测你时，使用你适用属性修正值中最低的那个。

章节：进行游戏Starfinder Player Core 第425页

Detecting with Other Senses

Most abilities that designate "a creature you can see" or the like function just as well if the user can precisely sense the subject with a different sense. If a monster uses a sense other than vision, the GM can adapt ways of avoiding detection that work with the monster's senses. For example, a creature that has echolocation might use hearing as a primary sense. This could mean its quarry is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} in a noisy chamber, @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} in a great enough din, or @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.zJxUflt9np0q4yML]{Invisible} under a @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.spells.Item.gIdDLrbswTV3OBJy]{Silence} spell.

Using Stealth with Other Senses

The Stealth skill is designed to use Hide for avoiding visual detection and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.IE2nThCmoyhQA0Jn]{Avoid Notice} and @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.VMozDqMMuK5kpoX4]{Sneak} to avoid being both seen and heard. For many special senses, a player can describe how they're avoiding detection by that special sense and use the most applicable Stealth action. For instance, a creature stepping lightly to avoid being detected via tremorsense would be using Sneak.

In some cases, rolling a Dexterity-based Stealth skill check to Sneak doesn't make the most sense. For example, a PC trying to avoid being detected by a creature that senses heartbeats might meditate to slow their heart rate, using Wisdom instead of Dexterity for their Stealth check. When a creature could detect you using multiple different senses, use your lowest applicable attribute modifier.

Section: Playing the GameStarfinder Player Core pg. 425

Exploration Activities.name: 探索行动 Exploration Activities
Exploration Activities.text:
除“疾行”外，使用以下任一活动时你都必须以半速移动。

分析环境

专注

扫描环境危险。
避免注意使用隐匿来避免被注意到。
防御在你的第一个回合开始前举起护盾。
侦测魔法

专注

以固定间隔侦测魔法。
跟随专家

听觉

专注

视觉

从专家盟友处获得一项技能的加值。若未受训，则将你的等级加入；并获得+2情境加值（若盟友为大师则为+3，若盟友为传奇则为+4）。
疾行

移动

以双倍速度移动，持续最多体质调整值 × 10分钟。
调查

专注

使用回忆知识来发现线索。
直播

专注

直播或录制你的冒险。
充能

操作

为一块耗尽的电池充能。
重复施放法术

专注

反复施放法术或维持一个法术。
侦察

专注

盟友的先攻检定获得+1情境加值。
搜索

专注

寻找隐藏门和危险。
维持一个效果

专注

维持一个可被维持的效果。

Bulk and Encumbered.name: 负载和超载 Bulk and Encumbered
Bulk and Encumbered.text:
负载

携带特别沉重或难以摆弄的物品会使你更难移动，携带过多装备使自己超载也是如此。物品的负载值反映了该物品有多难处理，代表其尺寸、重量以及整体上的累赘程度。如果你的力量调整值很高，通常你不必担心负载，除非你携带了大量相当沉重的物品。

生物体型负载
超小型1
小型3
中型6
大型12
超大型24
巨型48

不同体型的负载换算

如下表所示，大型或更大的生物相比小型或中型生物，较少受到笨重物品的妨碍，而超小型生物则更快变得不堪重负。大型生物将 10 件 1 负载的物品视为 1 负载，超大型生物将 10 件 2 负载的物品视为 1 负载，依此类推。超小型生物将 10 件可忽略负载的物品视为 1 负载。可忽略物品的运作方式也类似——超大型生物将 1 负载的物品视为可忽略，因此它可以携带任意数量的 1 负载物品。超小型生物不会将任何物品视为具有可忽略负载。

超载

你携带的重量超过了你所能承受的范围。当你处于超载状态时，你会获得 @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{笨拙 1}，并且你的所有速度都承受 –10 英尺减值。和所有对你速度的减值一样，这不能使你的速度降低到 5 英尺以下。

章节：进行游戏《Starfinder 玩家核心》 第 235 页

Bulk

Carrying especially heavy or unwieldy items can make it more difficult for you to move, as can overloading yourself with too much gear. The Bulk value of an item reflects how difficult the item is to handle, representing its size, weight, and general awkwardness. If you have a high Strength modifier, you usually don't need to worry about Bulk unless you're carrying numerous substantial items.

Size of CreatureBulk
Tiny1
Small3
Medium6
Large12
Huge24
Gargantuan48

Bulk Conversions for Different Sizes

As shown in the table below, Large or larger creatures are less encumbered by bulky items than Small or Medium creatures, while Tiny creatures become overburdened more quickly. A Large creature treats 10 items of 1 Bulk as 1 Bulk, a Huge creature treats 10 items of 2 Bulk as 1 Bulk, and so on. A Tiny creature treats 10 items of negligible Bulk as 1 Bulk. Negligible items work in a similar way—a Huge creature treats items of 1 Bulk as negligible, so it can carry any number of items of 1 Bulk. A Tiny creature doesn't treat any items as having negligible Bulk.

Encumbered

You are carrying more weight than you can manage. While you're encumbered, you're @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} and take a –10-foot penalty to all your Speeds. As with all penalties to your Speed, this can't reduce your Speed below 5 feet.

Hero Points.name: 英雄点 Hero Points
Hero Points.text:
在每次游戏开始时，给每位PC发放1点英雄点。之后大约每游玩1小时，再发放约1点，奖励英勇行为或中等、重大成就。英雄点可以通过两种方式使用。

花费1点英雄点来重骰一次检定，并采用第二次结果。这是一个幸运效果。

花费你全部的英雄点，以在你的濒死状态将要增加时避免死亡。失去濒死状态并以0 HP稳定。以这种方式失去濒死状态时，你不会因此获得或增加受创值，但如果你原本已经有该状态，则不会失去它。

章节：进行游戏《Starfinder Player Core》页405

Give out 1 Hero Point to each PC at the start of the session. Give out roughly 1 more per hour of play, for a heroic act or a moderate or major accomplishment. Hero Points can be spent in two ways.

Spend 1 Hero Point to reroll a check and use the second result. This is a fortune effect.

Spend all your Hero Points to avoid death when your dying condition would increase. Lose dying and stabilize with 0 HP. Don't gain or increase your wounded value from losing the dying condition in this way, but if you already had that condition you don't lose it.

Section: Playing the GameStarfinder Player Core pg. 405

Monster Adjustments.name: 怪物调整 Monster Adjustments
Monster Adjustments.text:
菁英调整

  • 将该生物的等级提高 1；如果该生物的等级为 –1 或 0，则改为将其等级提高 2。
  • AC、攻击加值、DC、豁免、察觉和技能全部增加 2。
  • 打击和攻击性能力的伤害增加 2；对于只能在有限次数内使用的能力，则增加 4。

  • 根据该生物的初始等级提高 HP：1 或更低时，增加 10 HP；2 到 4 时，增加 15 HP；5 到 19 时，增加 20 HP；20+ 时，增加 30 HP。

虚弱调整

  • 将该生物的等级降低 1；如果该生物的等级为 1，则改为将其等级降低 2。
  • AC、攻击加值、DC、豁免、察觉和技能全部减少 2。
  • 打击和攻击性能力的伤害减少 2；对于只能在有限次数内使用的能力，则减少 4。

  • 根据该生物的初始等级降低 HP：1 或 2 时，减少 10 HP；3 到 5 时，减少 15 HP；6 到 20 时，减少 20 HP；21+ 时，减少 30 HP。

章节：运行游戏Starfinder Alien Core 第 204、207 页

Elite Adjustment

  • Increase the creature's level by 1; if the creature is level –1 or 0, instead increase its level by 2.
  • Add 2 to AC, attack bonus, DCs, saves, Perception, and skills.
  • Add 2 to damage for Strikes and offensive abilities, or 4 for abilities that can be used only a limited number of times.

  • Increase HP based on the creature's starting level: 1 or lower, add 10 HP; 2 to 4, add 15 HP; 5 to 19, add 20 HP; and 20+ add 30 HP.

Weak Adjustment

  • Decrease the creature's level by 1; if the creature is level 1, instead decrease its level by 2.
  • Subtract 2 from AC, attack bonus, DCs, saves, Perception, and skills.
  • Subtract 2 from damage for Strikes and offensive abilities, or 4 for abilities that can be used only a limited number of times.

  • Decrease HP based on the creature's starting level: 1 or 2, subtract 10 HP; 3 to 5, subtract 15 HP; 6 to 20, subtract 20 HP; and 21+ subtract 30 HP.

Section: Running the GameStarfinder Alien Core pg. 204, 207

Earn Income Tasks.name: 赚取收入任务 Earn Income Tasks
Earn Income Tasks.text:
艺术、图书馆、其他教育类学识
在图书馆、博物馆或学校工作
为一次远征整理另一个世界的资料
在信息圈上进行讲座
为收藏家获取一件早于大断层时期的稀有物品
植入体学识
尝试开发新的植入体或翻新旧植入体
自愿试用实验性植入体
帮助病人适应他们的新植入体
烹饪学识
参加烹饪比赛
在信息圈上直播烹饪教学
寻找无法合成的稀有外星食材
企业学识
在一家大型企业获得入门级工作
成为大型企业的招聘人员或经理
为出价最高者进行企业间谍活动
制作
制作手工艺品并在信息圈上出售
为前往展会的网红缝制服装
在本地废品场搜寻还能用的电子设备
赌博学识
在赌场里走运一把
为本地暴徒球联赛经营赌池
经营一个收购、开箱并出售收藏品的网站
信息圈学识
担任 IT 专业人员，帮助人们处理科技问题
在信息圈上寻找优惠交易，再转卖物品牟利
在信息圈上创建、托管或管理内容
法律学识
处理一些小规模的繁文缛节
通过法律体系将一家企业绳之以法
找出由魔鬼撰写的最终用户许可协议中的漏洞
生命科学学识
帮助照料本地收容所里的珍奇动物
在本地大学做一场关于异种生物学的讲座
开发一种为特定生物工程植入体进行无痛安装的新方法
采矿学识
在本地水晶矿工作一个班次
确定一种复合星铁的精确成分
协助制定开采一颗新发现小行星的方案
表演
在街角演奏视游主题曲
在信息圈上直播
获得一份为流行歌星担任伴唱的工作
海盗学识
向本地海盗出售航运信息
走私并出售赃物而不被抓住
辨认船员徽记并回忆海盗活动的历史
体育学识
成为职业暴徒球联赛的一名选手
在体育场馆前出售周边商品或倒卖门票
帮助运动员进行体能调整和训练
黑社会学识
追查失窃物品或失踪人员
与犯罪辛迪加首领达成交易
将一批盗版货物走私进港口
视游学识
作为职业视游玩家直播游戏
设计、开发并编写你自己的视游程序
收费提供“装备代刷”服务，帮助人们通关一款热门游戏
战争学识
在道场教授一门多什科战斗课程
以雇佣兵或受雇枪手的身份加入冲突的一方
训练舰队军官进行军事机动与战术策划

章节：运行游戏《星际探险家 GM 核心》47-48页

Art, Library, Other Educational Lore
Work at a library, museum, or school
Compile information on another world for an expedition
Conduct lectures on the infosphere
Acquire a rare object that predates the Gap for a collector
Augmentation Lore
Try to develop new augmentations or refurbish old ones
Volunteer to try experimental augmentations
Help patients get used to their new augmentations
Cooking Lore
Compete in a cooking competition
Livestream cooking tutorials on the infosphere
Find rare alien ingredients that can't be synthesized
Corporate Lore
Get an entry-level job at a major corporation
Become a recruiter or manager for a major corporation
Conduct corporate espionage for the highest bidder
Crafting
Make handicrafts to sell on the infosphere
Sew a costume for an influencer going to a convention
Scavenge a local junkyard for working electronics
Gambling Lore
Get lucky in a casino
Run a betting pool for a local brutaris league
Operate a site that buys, opens, and sells collectibles
Infosphere Lore
Work as an IT professional helping people with tech
Find deals on the infosphere and resell the items for profit
Create, host, or administrate content on the infosphere
Legal Lore
Clear some minor red tape
Bring a corporation to justice through the legal system
Find loopholes in an EULA written by a devil
Life Science Lore
Help care for exotic animals at a local shelter
Give a lecture at a local university about xenobiology
Develop a new painless way to install a specific biotech augmentation
Mining Lore
Work a shift in a local crystal mine
Determine the exact composition of a composite starmetal
Help develop a plan to mine out a newly discovered asteroid
Performance
Play vidgame theme songs on a street corner
Livestream on the infosphere
Get a gig as a backup singer for a pop star
Piracy Lore
Sell shipping information to local pirates
Smuggle and sell stolen goods without getting caught
Recognize crew emblems and recall the history of piracy
Sports Lore
Become a player in a professional brutaris league
Sell merchandise or scalp tickets in front of a sports arena
Help condition and train athletes
Underworld Lore
Track down stolen items or missing people
Make a deal with the head of a crime syndicate
Smuggle a shipment of pirated goods into a port
Vidgame Lore
Livestream games as a professional vidgamer
Design, develop, and program your own vidgame
Charge for “gear runs” to help people beat a popular game
Warfare Lore
Teach a doshko fighting class at a dojo
Join one side of a conflict as a mercenary or gun for hire
Train fleet officers in military maneuvers and stratagems

Section: Running the GameStarfinder GM Core pg. 47-48

Downtime Events.name: 休整期事件 Downtime Events
Downtime Events.text:
制作或挣取收入（工艺）
一批重要材料的货运延误了，PC必须追查其下落。
PC创作出一件极为出色的作品，从而引起了一名收藏家或盗贼的注意。
制作伪造品（社群）
PC正试图仿造的法律格式发生了变化，而他们必须作出调整。
一位神秘的赞助人赠予PC特殊情报，但暗示他们日后会要求PC回报一个人情。
挣取收入（通用）
一位挑剔的客户在整个过程中都要求进行重大修改。
工地上的一次事故让某人陷入危险。
PC正在进行的某项工作突然成了风潮或爆款——需求暴涨！
一名游客对PC的工作印象深刻，并向他们提供了一项报酬更高、地点在遥远星球上的任务。
工作现场的条件糟糕透顶，其他工人请求PC加入他们，一同对抗那家企业。
挣取收入（表演）
PC开启了他们的首次巡演，变得比以往任何时候都更忙碌、更受欢迎！
一个热门的信息圈频道为一场特别演出提供资金，但要求对内容做出一些改动。
PC的一位同台表演者没有到场，但演出必须继续！
维生（求生）
长期在同一区域维生时，PC发现了一种稀有浆果或草药，可能有助于制作一种新药。
PC发现了一些迹象，表明某种大型生物也一直在这里觅食——有可能是怪物。
编程（计算机）
你们计算机系统的一次更新，使你的程序在任何连接到信息圈的系统上都变得不稳定。
你需要帮助来完成一个程序，而唯一愿意帮忙的程序员有着可疑的背景。
一家巨型企业偷走了你的创意，并要求你停止运营，除非你能证明这个计算机程序是被盗的。
买卖物品
PC买入或卖出了一件赃物，随后被那个不惜一切代价也要将其追回的组织追捕。
一名商贩卖给PC一件危险的假货。
一名线上商家有PC想要的物品，但不愿发货，要求他们亲自去取并将其走私回家。
重训
接触新的魔法力量会招致诅咒，或引发一种古怪现象。
PC们一直用来训练的视频其实是骗人的，结果导致他们在体能训练中受了伤。

章节：运行游戏《Starfinder GM Core》48页

Craft or Earn Income (Crafting)
A shipment of important materials is delayed, and the PC must track it down.
The PC creates a superlative work, which draws the attention of a collector or thief.
Create a Forgery (Society)
The legal format the PC is attempting to mimic gets changed, and they must adjust.
A mysterious benefactor gifts the PC special information but suggests they'll ask for a favor later to reciprocate.
Earn Income (General)
A fussy client demands dramatic changes throughout the process.
An accident at a work site puts someone in danger.
Something the PC is working on becomes a fad or hit— demand skyrockets!
A tourist is impressed with the PC's work and offers them a more lucrative task on a faraway planet.
Conditions on the job site are abysmal, and other workers ask the PC to join them in confronting the corporation.
Earn Income (Performance)
The PC launches their debut tour, becoming busier and more popular than ever!
A popular infosphere channel finances a special performance but demands some changes to the contents.
One of the PC's fellow performers doesn't show up, but the show must go on!
Subsist (Survival)
Over a long time subsisting in a single area, the PC finds a rare berry or herb that could be useful for making a new medicine.
The PC finds signs indicating some large creature has been foraging as well—possibly a monster.
Program (Computers)
An update to your computer systems makes your program unstable on any system connected to the infosphere.
You need help finishing a program, and the only programmer willing to help has a shady background.
A megacorporation steals your idea and demands you cease your operations unless you can prove the computer program was stolen.
Buy and Sell Items
The PC buys or sells a stolen item and is pursued by the organization looking to get it back at any cost.
A vendor sells the PC a dangerous fake item.
An online vendor has the item the PC wants but won't ship it, requiring them to pick it up and smuggle it back home.
Retrain
Tapping into new magical powers inflicts a curse or creates an odd phenomenon.
The vids the PCs have been using to train were fraudulent and resulted in an injury during their physical training.

Subsystems and Variant Rules.name: 子系统和可选规则 Subsystems and Variant Rules
Subsystems and Variant Rules.text:
来源：

  • 《Starfinder GM Core》

  • 《Pathfinder Gamemastery Guide》

Sources:

  • Starfinder GM Core

  • Pathfinder Gamemastery Guide

Automatic Bonus Progression.name: 自动成长加值 Automatic Bonus Progression
Automatic Bonus Progression.text:
这个变体移除了通常由魔法物品提供给掷骰和DC的物品加值（护甲的物品加值除外），并用一种新的加值——潜能——来替代，以反映角色的天赋能力。在这个变体中，魔法物品如果确实存在，也会提供独特的特殊能力，而非数值提升。

特殊职业特性

每个角色都会自动获得“自动加值成长”表上的职业特性。

自动加值成长

等级收益
1-
2攻击潜能 +1
3技能潜能（一项为 +1）
4毁灭性攻击（两颗骰子）
5防御潜能 +1
6技能潜能（两项各为 +1）
7察觉潜能 +1
8豁免检定潜能 +1
9技能潜能（一项为 +2，一项为 +1）
10攻击潜能 +2
11防御潜能 +2
12毁灭性攻击（三颗骰子）
13察觉潜能 +2；技能潜能（两项各为 +2，一项为 +1）
14豁免检定潜能 +2
15技能潜能（三项各为 +2，一项为 +1）
16攻击潜能 +3
17属性巅峰；技能潜能（一项为 +3，两项各为 +2，两项各为 +1）
18防御潜能 +3
19毁灭性攻击（四颗骰子），察觉潜能 +3
20豁免检定潜能 +3；技能潜能（两项各为 +3，两项各为 +2，两项各为 +1）

攻击潜能2级

从2级开始，你使用所有武器和徒手攻击进行攻击检定时获得 +1 潜能加值。该加值在10级时提升为 +2，并在16级时提升为 +3。

技能潜能3级

在3级时，选择一项技能。你在该技能上获得 +1 潜能加值。在6级时，选择第二项技能，使其获得 +1 潜能加值。在9级时，从这两项技能中选择一项，将其潜能加值提升至 +2。在13级时，将第二项技能的潜能加值提升至 +2，并选择第三项技能，使其获得 +1 潜能加值。在15级时，将第三项技能的潜能加值提升至 +2，并选择第四项技能，使其获得 +1 潜能加值。在17级时，从你三项具有 +2 潜能加值的技能中选择一项，将其提升至 +3，并选择第五项技能，使其获得 +1 潜能加值。最后，在20级时，从两项具有 +2 潜能加值的技能中选择一项，将其提升至 +3；从三项具有 +1 潜能加值的技能中选择一项，将其提升至 +2；并选择一项新技能，使其获得 +1 潜能加值。你可以在任何时候花费1周时间重训这些分配中的一项。

毁灭性攻击4级

在4级时，你的武器和徒手打击造成两颗伤害骰，而非一颗。该效果在12级时提升为三颗，并在19级时提升为四颗。

防御潜能5级

在5级时，你获得对AC的 +1 潜能加值。在11级时，该加值提升为 +2；在18级时，提升为 +3。

察觉潜能7级

在7级时，你获得对察觉的 +1 潜能加值，在13级时提升为 +2，在19级时提升为 +3。

豁免检定潜能8级

在8级时，你的豁免获得 +1 潜能加值，在14级时提升为 +2，在20级时提升为 +3。

属性巅峰17级

在17级时，选择一个属性调整值，使其增加1点，或提升至 +4（取能提供更高调整值的结果）。

调整物品与宝藏

使用这个变体时，你可以按自己意愿忽略第59页“队伍分级宝藏”表中的大部分内容，不过你通常仍会希望提供稳定的货币收入。你的选择影响最大的主要领域是施法物品，例如卷轴和魔杖。移除所有潜能符文、强击符文和坚韧符文。通常会给予属性或伤害骰物品加值的物品不再提供这些加值，护甲提供的AC基础物品加值除外。巅峰物品不会提升属性调整值。如果你的世界中仍然有魔法物品，一个稳妥的做法是继续发放消耗品。如果你选择彻底移除符文，这可能会降低PC的伤害，因为他们将无法获得如 flaming 或 holy 之类的符文。如果你移除了几乎所有宝藏，那么即使有自动加值，挑战也可能会变得更加困难。

章节：子系统与变体规则《星际探险者 GM Core》83页

This variant removes the item bonus to rolls and DCs usually provided by magic items (with the exception of armor's item bonus) and replaces it with a new kind of bonus—potency—to reflect a character's innate ability. In this variant, magic items, if they exist at all, can provide unique special abilities rather than numerical increases.

Special Class Features

Every character automatically gains the class features on the Automatic Bonus Progression table.

AUTOMATIC BONUS PROGRESSION

LevelBenefits
1-
2Attack potency +1
3Skill potency (one at +1)
4Devastating attacks (two dice)
5Defense potency +1
6Skill potency (two at +1 each)
7Perception potency +1
8Saving throw potency +1
9Skill potency (one at +2, one at +1)
10Attack potency +2
11Defense potency +2
12Devastating attacks (three dice)
13Perception potency +2; skill potency (two at +2 each, one at +1)
14Saving throw potency +2
15Skill potency (three at +2 each, one at +1)
16Attack potency +3
17Attribute apex; skill potency (one at +3, two at +2 each, two at +1 each)
18Defense potency +3
19Devastating attacks (four dice), Perception potency +3
20Saving throw potency +3; skill potency (two at +3 each, two at +2 each, two at +1 each)

Attack Potency2nd

Starting at 2nd level, you gain a +1 potency bonus to attack rolls with all weapons and unarmed attacks. This increases to +2 at 10th level, and +3 at 16th level.

Skill Potency3rd

At 3rd level, choose a single skill. You gain a +1 potency bonus with that skill. At 6th level, choose a second skill to gain a +1 potency bonus. At 9th level, choose one of those skills and increase its potency bonus to +2. At 13th level, increase the potency bonus of your second skill to +2 and choose a third skill to gain a +1 potency bonus. At 15th level, increase the third skill's potency bonus to +2 and choose a fourth skill to gain a +1 potency bonus. At 17th level, choose one of your three skills with a +2 potency bonus to increase to +3, and choose a fifth skill to gain a +1 potency bonus. Finally, at 20th level, choose one of the two skills with a +2 potency bonus to increase to +3, choose one of the three skills at a +1 potency bonus to increase to +2, and choose one new skill to gain a +1 potency bonus. You can spend 1 week to retrain one of these assignments at any time.

Devastating Attacks4th

At 4th level, your weapon and unarmed Strikes deal two damage dice instead of one. This increases to three at 12th level and to four at 19th level.

Defense Potency5th

At 5th level, you gain a +1 potency bonus to AC. At 11th level, this bonus increases to +2, and at 18th level, to +3.

Perception Potency7th

At 7th level, you gain a +1 potency bonus to Perception, increasing to +2 at level 13 and +3 at level 19.

Saving Throw Potency8th

At 8th level, you gain a +1 potency bonus to saves, increasing to +2 at level 14 and +3 at level 20.

Attribute Apex17th

At 17th level, choose one attribute modifier to either increase by 1 or increase to +4 (whichever grants the higher modifer).

Adjusting Items and Treasure

With this variant, you can ignore as much of the Party Treasure by Level table on page 59 as you want, though you'll usually want to provide consistent currency. The main area your choice will impact is in spellcasting items, such as scrolls and wands. Remove all potency runes, striking runes, and resilient runes. Items that normally grant an item bonus to statistics or damage dice no longer do, other than the base item bonus to AC from armor. Apex items do not increase attribute modifiers. If your world still includes magic items, a safe bet is to continue to give out consumable items. If you choose to eliminate runes entirely, this can reduce the PCs' damage since they won't have runes like flaming or holy. If you've removed nearly all treasure, challenges might become more difficult, even with automatic bonuses.

Section: Subsystem and Variant RulesStarfinder GM Core pg. 83

Free Archetype.name: 免费变体 Free Archetype
Free Archetype.text:
有时，你的游戏故事会需要一个所有人都是海盗，或都是魔法学校学徒的团队。自由原型变体会为每个角色引入一个共同特征，同时不会剥夺该角色现有的任何选择。

构建角色

普通角色与自由原型角色之间唯一的区别在于，角色会在2级以及此后每个偶数等级获得一个额外的职业专长，而这个专长只能用于原型专长。你可以将这些免费专长限制为团队中每个角色都拥有的单一原型的专长（用于体现共同背景故事）、符合某一特定主题的原型专长（例如在以魔法学校为背景的游戏中，仅限魔法原型），或者如果你只是想要一个更高强度的游戏，也可以完全不作限制。

如果团队全都拥有相同的原型，或是从一份有限的列表中选择原型，你可能会想忽略自由原型通常要求在选择新原型前必须先选取一定数量专长的限制。这样一来，角色仍然可以继续发展另一个同样符合其角色设定的原型。

使用自由原型进行游戏

自由原型角色比普通角色稍微更多才多艺，也更强一些，但通常还不至于破坏你游戏的平衡。不过，由于角色能更容易获得原型专长，你应当对那些效果会根据角色拥有的原型专长数量而提升的专长数量设定上限（主要是多职业“坚韧”专长）。允许角色从这些专长中受益的数量等于其等级的一半，是较为合适的。

章节：子系统与变体规则Starfinder GM Core pg. 84

Sometimes the story of your game calls for a group where everyone is a pirate or an apprentice at a magic school. The free archetype variant introduces a shared aspect to every character without taking away any of that character's existing choices.

Building a Character

The only difference between a normal character and a free-archetype character is that the character receives an extra class feat at 2nd level and every even level thereafter that they can use only for archetype feats. You might restrict the free feats to those of a single archetype each character in the group has (for a shared backstory), those of archetypes fitting a certain theme (such as only ones from magical archetypes in a game set in a magic school), or entirely unrestricted if you just want a higher-powered game.

If the group all has the same archetype or draws from a limited list, you might want to ignore the free archetype's normal restriction of selecting a certain number of feats before taking a new archetype. That way a character can still pursue another archetype that also fits their character.

Playing with Free Archetypes

Free-archetype characters are a bit more versatile and powerful than normal, but usually not so much that they unbalance your game. However, due to the characters' increased access to archetype feats, you should place a limit on the number of feats that scale based on a character's number of archetype feats (mainly multiclass Resiliency feats). Allowing a character to benefit from a number of these feats equal to half their level is appropriate.

Section: Subsystem and Variant RulesStarfinder GM Core pg. 84

Gradual Attribute Boosts.name: 渐进属性提升 Gradual Attribute Boosts
Gradual Attribute Boosts.text:
在这个变体规则中，角色会随着升级更循序渐进地获得属性增强，而不是在5级、10级、15级和20级时一次获得四次属性增强。每名角色在达到2级、3级、4级和5级时，各获得一次属性增强。这些共同构成一组属性增强，因此角色在同一组中不能将同一属性值增强超过一次；玩家可以在每个已增强的属性值旁边点一个点，或用其他方式做标记以便追踪。PC也会在7级、8级、9级和10级时各获得一次属性增强（第二组）；在12级、13级、14级和15级时（第三组）；以及在17级、18级、19级和20级时（第四组，也是最后一组）。

这会将属性增强分散开来，而更早使用这些增强意味着角色可以在更低等级时提高自己最重要的属性调整值。这样会让角色平均而言略微更强，但也会让5级、10级、15级和20级显得没那么重要，因为角色通常会在这些等级选择该组中最不重要的那次属性增强。

章节：子系统与变体规则Pathfinder Gamemastery Guide 第182页

In this variant, a character gains attribute boosts more gradually as they level up, rather than receiving four attribute boosts at 5th, 10th, 15th, and 20th levels. Each character gains one attribute boost when they reach each of 2nd, 3rd, 4th, and 5th levels. These are collectively a single set of attribute boosts, so a character can't boost the same attribute score more than once per set; players can put a dot next to each boosted attribute score or otherwise mark it to keep track. PCs also receive an attribute boost at 7th, 8th, 9th, and 10th level (a second set); at 12th, 13th, 14th, and 15th level (a third set); and at 17th, 18th, 19th, and 20th level (the fourth and final set).

This spreads out the attribute boosts, and using them earlier means a character can increase their most important attribute modifiers at a lower level. This makes characters slightly more powerful on average, but it makes levels 5, 10, 15, and 20 less important since characters usually choose the least important attribute boost of the set at those levels.

Section: Subsystem and Variant RulesPathfinder Gamemastery Guide pg. 182

Proficiency without Level.name: 无关等级的熟练 Proficiency without Level
Proficiency without Level.text:
这个变体会将角色等级从其熟练加值中移除，以不同的方式进行成长，用于一种偏离常规的游戏风格。这是对系统的一项重大改动。Player Core 中的熟练等级成长，是为那种英雄从卑微出身一路成长为传奇强者的科学幻想游戏而设计的。但对某些游戏来说，这种叙事弧线并不合适。这类游戏讲述的是：在一个充满不确定性且残酷的世界中谨慎权衡、步步为营，在那里，即使是世界上最优秀的士兵，也无法保证能战胜一大群中等熟练程度的雇佣兵。

其初始实现相当直接：熟练加值改为受训 +2、专家 +4、大师 +6、传奇 +8。对于未受训角色，最好给予 –2 的熟练调整值，而不是 +0 的熟练加值。

此外，对于生物、危害、魔法物品等，将所有本会包含熟练加值的统计值，都按该生物或其他规则要素的等级予以降低。这些统计值通常包括攻击、AC、豁免检定、察觉、技能和法术的调整值与 DC。

最后，降低大多数任务的技能 DC。你可以直接从第 53 页的 DC 表中减去等级，或者参考“简易技能 DC（无等级）”表，使用一组更容易记忆的 DC。与使用默认数值相比，这些新的 DC 会让高等级角色成功变得稍微更难一些，这也符合前文提到的主题。战斗结果将趋于平缓，整个游戏中的严重成功和严重失败都会更少见。这一点在法术上尤其明显，因为你将更少看到豁免严重失败所带来的极端效果。

表：简易技能 DC（无等级）
熟练等级DC
未受训10
受训15
专家20
大师25
传奇30

调整遭遇战

如果你想讲述这样的故事：一大群低等级怪物对高等级 PC 仍然构成重大威胁（反过来说，一只更高等级的怪物对一群 PC 却算不上太大威胁），那么在构建遭遇战时就需要做出一些重大调整，其中也包括对 PC 奖励的调整。

在默认数学模型下，两个某一等级的怪物，大致相当于一只高出 2 级的怪物所带来的挑战。然而，在将等级从熟练中移除后，这一假设便不再成立。生物的 XP 预算会改用不同的尺度，如“生物 XP（无等级）”表所示。对于特定威胁等级，你仍然使用与 @UUID[.WZgFEMDSP9TvYc4u]{遭遇战预算} 中所示相同的 XP 预算（例如，中等威胁遭遇战为 80 XP，重度威胁遭遇战为 120 XP，等等）。

表：生物 XP（无等级）

生物等级

XP
队伍等级 - 79
队伍等级 - 612
队伍等级 - 514
队伍等级 - 418
队伍等级 - 321
队伍等级 - 226
队伍等级 - 132
队伍等级40
队伍等级 + 148
队伍等级 + 260
队伍等级 + 372

队伍等级 + 6135
队伍等级 + 7160

虽然“生物 XP（无等级）”表中的 XP 数值在大多数情况下都运作良好，但有时在面对等级差异极大的队伍时，它们可能无法充分反映生物特殊能力的影响。例如，一个幽灵法师即使寡不敌众，也可能凭借其虚体、飞行和高阶法术，对 5 级 PC 构成超出预期的威胁。

调整财宝

标准生物身上的物品选择，通常是为了避免在 PC 一般会与之交战的等级阶段发放过多财宝。然而，使用这个变体后，PC 可能会击败比自己高 5 级、甚至更高等级的生物！如果与高等级敌人的遭遇过多，最终可能会让 PC 获得比你预期更多的财宝；反之，如果他们对抗的是较弱但更难缠、却仍然只带有较差财宝的敌人，也可能出现相反情况。如果 PC 的财宝与预期偏离过远，你可以定期进行调整。如果让他们无法轻易出售或购买魔法物品，那么他们就更难利用自己获得的财宝来牟利。若想彻底回避这一问题，你可以使用自动加值成长。

章节：子系统与变体规则Starfinder GM Core 第 85 页

This variant removes a character's level from their proficiency bonus, scaling it differently for a style of game that's outside the norm. This is a significant change to the system. The proficiency rank progression in Player Core is designed for science-fantasy games where heroes rise from humble origins to legendary strength. For some games, this narrative arc doesn't fit. Such games are about hedging bets in an uncertain and gritty world, in which even the world's best soldier can't guarantee a win against a large group of moderately skilled mercenaries.

The initial implementation is fairly straightforward: the proficiency bonus just becomes +2 for trained, +4 for expert, +6 for master, and +8 for legendary. It's best to give an untrained character a –2 proficiency modifier instead of a +0 proficiency bonus.

Additionally, for creatures, hazards, magic items, and so on, reduce each statistic that would include a proficiency bonus by the level of the creature or other rules element. These statistics are typically modifiers and DCs for attacks, ACs, saving throws, Perception, skills, and spells.

Finally, decrease the skill DCs of most tasks. You can just subtract the level from the DC tables on page 53, or you can reference the Simple Skill DCs (No Level) table for a set of DCs that's easier to remember. The new DCs make it a little harder for high-level characters to succeed than it would be when using the default numbers, in keeping with the theme mentioned earlier. Combat outcomes will tend to flatten out, with critical successes and critical failures being less likely across the game. This is particularly notable in spells, where you're less likely to see the extreme effects of critical failures on saves.

TABLE: SIMPLE SKILL DCS (NO LEVEL)
Proficiency RankDC
Untrained10
Trained15
Expert20
Master25
Legendary30

Adjusting Encounters

Telling stories where a large group of low-level monsters can still be a significant threat to a high-level PC (and conversely, where a single higher-level monster is not much of a threat to a group of PCs) requires some significant shifts in encounter building, including shifts in the PCs' rewards.

Under the default math, two monsters of a certain level are roughly as challenging as a single monster 2 levels higher. However, with level removed from proficiency, this assumption is no longer true. The XP budget for creatures uses a different scale, as shown in the Creature XP (No Level) table. You'll still use the same XP budget for a given threat level as shown on the @UUID[.WZgFEMDSP9TvYc4u]{Encounter Budget} (80 XP for a moderate-threat encounter, 120 for a severe-threat encounter, and so on).

TABLE: CREATURE XP (NO LEVEL)

Creature's Level

XP
Party level - 79
Party level - 612
Party level - 514
Party level - 418
Party level - 321
Party level - 226
Party level - 132
Party level40
Party level + 148
Party level + 260
Party level + 372
Party level + 490
Party level + 5108
Party level + 6135
Party level + 7160

While the XP values in the Creature XP (No Level) table work well in most cases, sometimes they might not account for the effects of creatures' special abilities when facing a party of a drastically different level. For instance, a ghost mage could prove too much for 5th-level PCs with its incorporeality, flight, and high-rank spells, even though it's outnumbered.

Adjusting Treasure

Items on standard creatures are chosen to avoid giving out too much treasure for the level at which PCs will typically fight them. However, using this variant, PCs might defeat a creature 5 levels higher than they are, or even more! Too many encounters with higher-level foes can wind up giving the PCs more treasure than you expected, or vice versa if they're fighting weaker foes that put up more of a fight but still have poor treasure. You can make periodic adjustments if the PCs' treasure drifts too far from expectations. Making it so they can't easily sell or buy magic items will mean it's harder for them to exploit treasure they gain. To sidestep the issue entirely, you can use automatic bonus progression.

Section: Subsystem and Variant RulesStarfinder GM Core pg. 85

Victory Points.name: 胜利点数 Victory Points
Victory Points.text:
累计掷骰

大成功 PC获得2点胜利点数。

成功 PC获得1点胜利点数。

大失败 PC失去1点胜利点数。

这意味着，PC检定的结果通常会使队伍获得1点胜利点数或不获得任何胜利点数。不过，专精的PC有很大机会为队伍赢得2点胜利点数，而异想天开的计划也有相当大的可能让PC失去1点胜利点数。

递减掷骰

大成功 如果有可能挽回局势，PC获得1点胜利点数。否则，视为成功。

成功 PC避免失去任何胜利点数。

失败 PC失去1点胜利点数。

大失败 PC失去2点胜利点数。

表：胜利点数量级
挑战持续时间VP终点VP阈值
快速遭遇3-5-
长时间遭遇7-104
一场游戏流程的大部分时间15-255, 10, 15
整个冒险期间，支线15-205, 10, 15
整个冒险期间，主线25-5010, 20, 30, 40

章节：子系统与变体规则Starfinder GM Core 第206-208页

Accumulating Rolls

Critical Success The PCs gain 2 Victory Points.

Success The PCs gain 1 Victory Point.

Critical Failure The PCs lose 1 Victory Point.

This means that the result of a PC's check usually results in the party gaining either 1 or no Victory Points. However, specialized PCs have a solid chance of earning the party 2 Victory Points, and hare‑brained schemes have a fair chance of losing the PCs 1 Victory Point.

Diminishing Rolls

Critical Success If regaining ground is possible, the PCs gain 1 Victory Point. Otherwise, as success.

Success The PCs avoid losing any Victory Points.

Failure The PCs lose 1 Victory Point.

Critical Failure The PCs lose 2 Victory Points.
